title_en: Cooperative protein-ligand antioxidant activation: Sulforaphane binding to human serum immunoglobulin Genhances neuroprotection in spinal cord neurons via structural and functional modifications.
journal: Spectrochimica acta. Part A, Molecular and biomolecular spectroscopy
abstract_en: Oxidative stress plays a central role in the progression of neurodegenerative damage, including spinal cord injury (SCI), where excessive reactive oxygen species (ROS) production leads to neuronal dysfunction and cell death. Although small-molecule antioxidants such as sulforaphane exhibit neuroprotective potential, their clinical efficacy is often limited by instability and poor bioavailability. In this study, we explore a cooperative protein-ligand strategy using human serum immunoglobulin G (HIgG) as a dynamic scaffold for sulforaphane binding and antioxidant modulation. An integrated experimental and computational approach combining multispectroscopy, molecular docking, molecular dynamics (MD) simulations, and in vitro neuronal assays was employed to characterize binding mechanism and antioxidant activity. Spectroscopic analyses confirmed spontaneous and moderately binding of sulforaphane to HIgG, primarily driven by hydrogen bonding and van der Waals interactions within the Fab region. MD simulations and CD data revealed that sulforaphane binding induces subtle but significant local conformational rearrangements in HIgG, enhancing solvent accessibility and flexibility of aromatic and redox-active residues. Functionally, in vitro experiments using spinal cord neurons under H₂O₂-induced oxidative stress demonstrated that the HIgG-sulforaphane complex provides significantly greater cytoprotection and ROS suppression than either component alone. In conclusion, these findings reveal a cooperative antioxidant mechanism in which HIgG-sulforaphane complex contribute to improved neuroprotective activity. This work highlights protein-ligand cooperativity as a promising strategy for designing next-generation antioxidant systems.
para1: 這項研究對健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在神經退行性疾病的領域。氧化壓力被認為是導致神經損傷的重要因素，像是脊髓損傷等情況，過多的活性氧會造成神經細胞功能失常和死亡。研究顯示，雖然一些小分子抗氧化劑如蘿蔔硫素具有神經保護潛力，但它們在臨床上的效果往往受到不穩定性和生物利用度低的限制。因此，尋找新的抗氧化策略對於改善神經健康至關重要。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討如何利用人類血清免疫球蛋白G（HIgG）作為一種動態支架，來增強蘿蔔硫素的抗氧化效果。研究者關心的問題是，如何通過結合這兩種物質來提高抗氧化活性，進而改善神經細胞在氧化壓力下的生存能力。這項研究希望能找到一種新的合作性抗氧化機制，來對抗由氧化壓力引起的神經損傷。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用了多種方法來進行分析，包括多光譜技術、分子對接、分子動力學模擬和體外神經元測試。這些方法幫助研究者了解蘿蔔硫素與HIgG的結合機制及其抗氧化活性。研究中確認了蘿蔔硫素能夠自發且適度地與HIgG結合，主要是通過氫鍵和范德瓦耳斯力進行的。這些實驗的對象主要是脊髓神經元，並在氧化壓力的影響下進行測試。
para4: 主要的發現是，HIgG-蘿蔔硫素複合物在提供細胞保護和抑制活性氧方面，表現出比單獨使用任一成分更顯著的效果。這表明HIgG和蘿蔔硫素之間存在合作性的抗氧化機制，能夠增強神經保護活性。這項研究突顯了蛋白質-配體合作性作為設計新一代抗氧化系統的潛力，為未來的研究提供了新的方向。

title_en: Generative AI-driven enzyme engineering unlocks sustainable valorization of broccoli waste into sulforaphane.
abstract_en: Current production of sulforaphane (SFN), a health-promoting isothiocyanate, relies mainly on energy-intensive plant extraction or chemical synthesis, limiting sustainable large-scale manufacture. Here, a waste-to-value framework integrating generative artificial intelligence (AI), multi-scale modeling, and life-cycle assessment (LCA) was established to engineer a robust microbial biocatalyst for low-carbon valorization of broccoli waste. Genome mining identified a novel Lactobacillus-derived β-thioglucosidase as a promising scaffold. A ProteinMPNN-AlphaFold design pipeline targeting flexible loops near the active site yielded an improved variant with markedly enhanced catalytic performance and thermal robustness, with kcat increasing from 98.5 ± 3.2 to 421.3 ± 9.1 min<sup>-1</sup> and Km decreasing from 4.6 ± 0.2 to 0.9 ± 0.1 mM. Molecular dynamics and hybrid quantum mechanics/molecular mechanics (QM/MM) analyses indicated that loop rigidification, improved hydrophobic packing, and a reshaped electrostatic environment promoted transition-state stabilization and reduced the calculated activation barrier for SC bond cleavage from 19.2 to 17.5 kcal mol<sup>-1</sup>. When applied to homogenized broccoli residues in a heterogeneous solid-liquid system, the engineered enzyme showed high SFN yields and broad tolerance across pH and temperature conditions. Comparative LCA further revealed ∼70% and ∼60% reductions in global warming potential and cumulative energy demand, respectively, relative to conventional routes. As the first study to integrate AI-driven protein engineering with rigorous sustainability metrics for SFN production, this work offers a scalable paradigm for the green biomanufacturing of high-value natural products from agricultural residues.
para1: 這項研究對健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，蘿蔔硫素是一種具有健康促進作用的化合物，通常來自青花椰菜等植物。透過這項研究，我們可以看到如何利用廢棄物來生產這種有益的化合物，這不僅能減少環境負擔，還能促進可持續的生產方式。這樣的研究有助於未來開發出更環保的健康產品，並可能對改善人類健康有正面影響，尤其是在抗氧化和抗炎等方面。
para2: 這項研究的主題是如何從青花椰菜的廢棄物中有效提取蘿蔔硫素，並且探索利用人工智慧和其他技術來提升這一過程的可持續性。研究者關心的問題是目前蘿蔔硫素的生產方式過於依賴高能耗的植物提取或化學合成，這限制了其大規模生產的可行性。因此，研究旨在尋找更環保的替代方案，並評估其對環境的影響。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者首先進行基因組挖掘，發現了一種新型的乳酸菌衍生的β-硫葡萄糖苷酶，並將其作為改良的基礎。接著，利用人工智慧的設計流程，針對酶的活性位點附近的靈活環進行優化，最終獲得了一種改良的酶變體，顯示出顯著提升的催化性能和熱穩定性。這些改良使得酶在不同的pH值和溫度條件下都能有效運作，並能夠在固液混合系統中對青花椰菜殘渣進行處理。
para4: 主要的發現是，這種經過改良的酶在青花椰菜殘渣中能夠產生高產量的蘿蔔硫素，並且在各種環境條件下表現出良好的耐受性。此外，與傳統生產方法相比，這項研究的比較生命週期評估顯示，全球暖化潛力和累積能源需求分別減少了約70%和60%。這些結果顯示，結合人工智慧的蛋白質工程與可持續性評估，為從農業廢棄物中綠色生產高價值天然產品提供了一個可擴展的範式。
explanation_zh:
這項研究對健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，蘿蔔硫素是一種具有健康促進作用的化合物，通常來自青花椰菜等植物。透過這項研究，我們可以看到如何利用廢棄物來生產這種有益的化合物，這不僅能減少環境負擔，還能促進可持續的生產方式。這樣的研究有助於未來開發出更環保的健康產品，並可能對改善人類健康有正面影響，尤其是在抗氧化和抗炎等方面。

這項研究的主題是如何從青花椰菜的廢棄物中有效提取蘿蔔硫素，並且探索利用人工智慧和其他技術來提升這一過程的可持續性。研究者關心的問題是目前蘿蔔硫素的生產方式過於依賴高能耗的植物提取或化學合成，這限制了其大規模生產的可行性。因此，研究旨在尋找更環保的替代方案，並評估其對環境的影響。

在實驗中，研究者首先進行基因組挖掘，發現了一種新型的乳酸菌衍生的β-硫葡萄糖苷酶，並將其作為改良的基礎。接著，利用人工智慧的設計流程，針對酶的活性位點附近的靈活環進行優化，最終獲得了一種改良的酶變體，顯示出顯著提升的催化性能和熱穩定性。這些改良使得酶在不同的pH值和溫度條件下都能有效運作，並能夠在固液混合系統中對青花椰菜殘渣進行處理。

主要的發現是，這種經過改良的酶在青花椰菜殘渣中能夠產生高產量的蘿蔔硫素，並且在各種環境條件下表現出良好的耐受性。此外，與傳統生產方法相比，這項研究的比較生命週期評估顯示，全球暖化潛力和累積能源需求分別減少了約70%和60%。這些結果顯示，結合人工智慧的蛋白質工程與可持續性評估，為從農業廢棄物中綠色生產高價值天然產品提供了一個可擴展的範式。

title_en: Sulforaphane attenuates cisplatin‑induced acute kidney injury by inhibiting oxidative stress, inflammation and apoptosis via regulation of NRF2.
abstract_en: Cisplatin‑induced acute kidney injury (CI‑AKI) is one of the most common comorbidities in patients undergoing chemotherapy, notably limiting the clinical use of cisplatin. However, the pathogenesis of CI‑AKI remains to be fully elucidated. Sulforaphane (SFN), a NRF2 agonist, exhibits anti‑inflammatory, antioxidant and anti‑apoptotic effects, thus SFN exerts protective effects in kidney injury diseases. However, the possible role and underlying mechanisms of SFN in CI‑AKI remain ambiguous. An in vivo model of CI‑AKI was constructed using C57BL/6 mice that were administered a single intraperitoneal cisplatin injection (20 mg/kg) and conditionally treated with SFN (10 mg/kg). Serum creatinine (Scr) and blood urea nitrogen (BUN) levels were detected by biochemical analysis. Western blotting was performed to assess the expression of renal injury markers, as well as the apoptosis‑related proteins cleaved caspase‑3, caspase‑3, Bax and Bcl‑2. Furthermore, hematoxylin and eosin and periodic acid‑Schiff staining were employed to detect renal tissue lesions in mice, and TUNEL staining was used to evaluate the apoptosis of renal tissues in each group in vivo. Immunohistochemistry was used to assess the expression of inflammatory marker F4/80 in mouse renal tissues, and ELISA was used to detect the expressions of the inflammatory markers IL)‑1β, IL‑6 and tumor necrosis factor‑α (TNF‑α) in the serum of mice in each group. DCFH‑DA) analysis was used to detect reactive oxygen species (ROS) levels and biochemical analysis was used to evaluate the expression levels of malondialdehyde, superoxide dismutase and glutathione. Finally, western blotting and immunohistochemistry were performed to evaluate the expression of NRF2. An in vitro model of CI‑AKI was constructed using HK‑2 cells induced by cisplatin (10 µg/ml) that were conditionally treated with one or both of SFN (5 µM) and the NRF2 inhibitor ML385 (1.9 µM). Reverse transcription‑quantitative PCR was performed to evaluate the expression of NRF2. Cell Counting Kit‑8 assay was performed to assess the viability of HK‑2 cells in different groups, whereas flow cytometry was used to assess the apoptosis of HK‑2 cells in different groups. DCFH‑DA analysis was performed to evaluate the expression of ROS in different treatment groups. Furthermore, ELISA was used to evaluate the expressions of IL‑1β, IL‑6 and TNF‑α in each group. SFN notably decreased the serum levels of Scr and BUN and decreased the expression levels of kidney injury molecule‑1 and neutrophil gelatinase‑associated lipocalin in the cisplatin‑induced model group. Histopathological examination revealed attenuated renal structural damage and preserved tubular architecture in the SFN intervention group. Furthermore, SFN notably inhibited the apoptosis, inflammation and oxidative stress of renal tissues induced with cisplatin. Additionally, SFN markedly upregulated NRF2. In vitro, the NRF2 inhibitor ML385 partially attenuated the effects of SFN on the viability, apoptosis, inflammation and oxidative stress of HK‑2 model cells. The present study indicated that SFN exerted its nephroprotective effect through NRF2‑mediated anti‑inflammatory, antioxidant and anti‑apoptotic mechanisms, positioning SFN as a promising therapeutic candidate for clinical management of chemotherapy‑associated kidney injury.
para1: 這項研究可能對健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在化療過程中，許多病人會面臨由順鉑引起的急性腎損傷（CI-AKI）。這種情況不僅影響患者的生活品質，還限制了順鉑的臨床使用。因此，尋找能夠減輕或預防這種腎損傷的治療方法是非常重要的。研究中提到的蘿蔔硫素，因其抗發炎、抗氧化和抗凋亡的特性，可能成為一個有前景的治療選擇，幫助改善這些患者的腎臟健康。
para2: 這項研究主要關注的是蘿蔔硫素在順鉑引起的急性腎損傷中的作用及其潛在機制。研究者希望能夠深入了解蘿蔔硫素如何影響腎臟健康，特別是在化療患者中，這些患者常常因為使用順鉑而遭受腎臟損傷。透過這項研究，科學家們希望能夠揭示蘿蔔硫素在腎臟保護方面的具體作用，並為未來的臨床應用提供依據。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者使用了C57BL/6小鼠作為模型，這些小鼠接受了單次的順鉑注射，並在此基礎上進行了蘿蔔硫素的處理。研究者透過血清生化分析來檢測小鼠的血清肌酸酐和尿素氮水平，並使用西方印跡法來評估腎損傷標記物及凋亡相關蛋白的表達。此外，還利用組織學染色技術來觀察腎組織的損傷情況，並進行了多種生化分析來評估氧化壓力和發炎標記物的變化。
para4: 研究的主要發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素能顯著降低順鉑引起的小鼠血清肌酸酐和尿素氮的水平，並減少腎損傷相關標記物的表達。組織學檢查也顯示，蘿蔔硫素能減輕腎臟結構損傷，保護腎小管的完整性。此外，蘿蔔硫素還能抑制順鉑引起的腎組織凋亡、發炎和氧化壓力，並顯著上調NRF2的表達。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過NRF2介導的機制發揮其腎臟保護作用，為化療相關的腎損傷提供了一個潛在的治療選擇。
explanation_zh:
這項研究可能對健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在化療過程中，許多病人會面臨由順鉑引起的急性腎損傷（CI-AKI）。這種情況不僅影響患者的生活品質，還限制了順鉑的臨床使用。因此，尋找能夠減輕或預防這種腎損傷的治療方法是非常重要的。研究中提到的蘿蔔硫素，因其抗發炎、抗氧化和抗凋亡的特性，可能成為一個有前景的治療選擇，幫助改善這些患者的腎臟健康。

這項研究主要關注的是蘿蔔硫素在順鉑引起的急性腎損傷中的作用及其潛在機制。研究者希望能夠深入了解蘿蔔硫素如何影響腎臟健康，特別是在化療患者中，這些患者常常因為使用順鉑而遭受腎臟損傷。透過這項研究，科學家們希望能夠揭示蘿蔔硫素在腎臟保護方面的具體作用，並為未來的臨床應用提供依據。

在實驗中，研究者使用了C57BL/6小鼠作為模型，這些小鼠接受了單次的順鉑注射，並在此基礎上進行了蘿蔔硫素的處理。研究者透過血清生化分析來檢測小鼠的血清肌酸酐和尿素氮水平，並使用西方印跡法來評估腎損傷標記物及凋亡相關蛋白的表達。此外，還利用組織學染色技術來觀察腎組織的損傷情況，並進行了多種生化分析來評估氧化壓力和發炎標記物的變化。

研究的主要發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素能顯著降低順鉑引起的小鼠血清肌酸酐和尿素氮的水平，並減少腎損傷相關標記物的表達。組織學檢查也顯示，蘿蔔硫素能減輕腎臟結構損傷，保護腎小管的完整性。此外，蘿蔔硫素還能抑制順鉑引起的腎組織凋亡、發炎和氧化壓力，並顯著上調NRF2的表達。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過NRF2介導的機制發揮其腎臟保護作用，為化療相關的腎損傷提供了一個潛在的治療選擇。

這項研究中，科學家們使用小鼠來觀察蘿蔔硫素的效果。他們發現，當小鼠在接受順鉑後再給予蘿蔔硫素時，腎臟的損傷明顯減少，甚至改善了腎臟的整體健康。研究還顯示，蘿蔔硫素能降低腎臟損傷的標記物，並減少發炎和氧化壓力。  
  
這些發現讓我們對未來的治療選擇充滿期待！  
  
🔍 主要重點：  
- 蘿蔔硫素可能幫助保護腎臟，特別是在化療過程中  
- 減少腎臟損傷的標記物和發炎反應  
- 為未來的臨床應用提供了潛在的基礎

title_en: Sulforaphane attenuates oxidative stress and vascular remodeling in indium lung disease rats via mediating the NF-κB and Nrf2 pathways.
abstract_en: Indium lung disease is characterized by interstitial pneumonia, pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (PAP), pulmonary fibrosis, and lung cancer. However, this is insofar irreversible. The disease is believed to be caused by inhalation of indium and its compounds. We constructed a rat model of indium lung disease to explore the possible role of the NF-κB/Nrf2 pathway in indium‑tin oxide nanoparticle (Nano-ITO)-induced pulmonary injury and to reveal the regulatory mechanism. To integrate computational network toxicology and molecular docking with a rat model to identify core molecular targets and pathways underpinning ITO toxicity, and evaluate whether modulation of the NF-κB/Nrf2 axis by sulforaphane or all-trans retinoic acid mitigates Nano-ITO-induced lung injury. Toxicity prediction, target mining and integration with oxidative stress and vascular remodeling gene sets identified intersection targets that were analyzed by PPI, GO/KEGG/Reactome enrichment and topological analysis to select core nodes. Molecular docking probed interactions between ITO components and proteins in NF-κB/Nrf2 pathways. Next, we evaluated the NF-κB/Nrf2 signaling pathway to elucidate the molecular mechanism of sulforaphane in regulating inflammation, oxidative stress, vascular remodeling, vascular hyperplasia, PAP, cholesterol accumulation, and interstitial fibrosis in indium lung disease induced by Nano-ITO. Findings show that the NF-κB/Nrf2 signaling pathway is involved in Nano-ITO-induced vascular remodeling, cholesterol crystal formation, alveolar protein deposition, collagen hyperplasia, and interstitial fibrosis in rats. Sulforaphane can effectively attenuate Nano-ITO-induced inflammation, oxidative stress and vascular remodeling, as manifested by decreased expression of CD31, α-SMA, Collagen I, SP-A, SP-D, and inflammatory cytokine, via activating Nrf2 and inhibiting the NF-κB signaling pathway.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是針對因吸入銦及其化合物而引起的肺部疾病。這類疾病包括間質性肺炎、肺泡蛋白沉積症、肺纖維化和肺癌，這些疾病目前被認為是不可逆的。透過了解銦肺病的發病機制，或許能夠找到預防或減輕這些疾病的方法，尤其是針對環境中有害物質的暴露，這對於公共健康和環境保護都有重要意義。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討銦肺病的發病機制，特別是銦錫氧化物納米粒子（Nano-ITO）引起的肺部損傷。研究者希望了解NF-κB/Nrf2信號通路在這一過程中的角色，並揭示其調控機制。透過建立大鼠模型，研究者能夠模擬銦肺病的情況，並進一步分析相關的分子機制，以期找出可能的治療策略。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用了大鼠模型來模擬銦肺病，並結合計算網絡毒理學和分子對接技術，來識別與ITO毒性相關的核心分子靶點和通路。研究者評估了蘿蔔硫素或全反式維生素A酸對NF-κB/Nrf2通路的調節作用，並觀察這些物質是否能減輕Nano-ITO引起的肺部損傷。實驗中還進行了毒性預測、靶點挖掘及基因集的整合分析，以深入了解相關的生物學機制。
para4: 主要發現顯示，NF-κB/Nrf2信號通路在Nano-ITO引起的血管重塑、膽固醇結晶形成、肺泡蛋白沉積、膠原蛋白增生和間質性纖維化中扮演重要角色。研究結果指出，蘿蔔硫素能有效減輕Nano-ITO引起的炎症、氧化壓力和血管重塑，這些效果是透過激活Nrf2和抑制NF-κB信號通路來實現的。雖然這些結果顯示出潛在的治療效果，但仍需進一步研究以確認其臨床應用的可行性。

title_en: Sulforaphane prevents abdominal aortic aneurysm formation through the inhibition of phenotypic switching of vascular smooth muscle cells and inflammation via the activation of Nrf2.
abstract_en: Targeted therapy for the phenotypic switching of vascular smooth muscle cell(VSMC) is a critical strategy for mitigating disease progression in patients with abdominal aortic aneurysms (AAAs). Sulforaphane (SFN), a nuclear factor E2 factor-related factor (Nrf2) activator, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties and might regulate the phenotypic switching of VSMCs. However, the role of SFN in AAA formation is unknown. In this study, we explored the inhibitory effect of SFN on AAA formation and its underlying mechanisms. Quantitative PCR, western blotting and immunohistochemistry were used to determine biomarkers of the contractile state of VSMC expression and proinflammatory factor expression in cell and mouse AAA samples. SFN treatment and Nrf2 knockout were performed to investigate the role of SFN in AAA formation. In vivo VSMC Nrf2 conditional knockout experiments were performed to determine the critical role of Nrf2 in the protective effect of SFN on AAA. SFN alleviated AAA formation and inhibited VSMC phenotype switching and vascular inflammation. Mechanistically, SFN retained the contractile state of VSMCs and inhibited inflammation via the activation of Nrf2. Moreover, Nrf2 deficiency significantly counteracted the protective effect of SFN in inhibiting VSMC phenotype switching and macrophage activation, thereby promoting AAA formation. SFN inhibited AAA formation by suppressing VSMC phenotype switching and vascular inflammation. SFN is a potential effective treatment for AAA.

title_en: Hyaluronic acid and folic acid-modified mesoporous silica nanoparticles delivering sulforaphane suppress NSCLC via SPI1/miR-616-5p axis.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SF) suppresses NSCLC metastasis by modulating miR-616-5p, which is transcriptionally upregulated by the oncogenic transcription factor SPI1. However, the roles of SF, SPI1, and miR-616-5p in NSCLC metastasis remain unclear. In this study, we found that SPI1 expression in NSCLC tissues positively correlated with miR-616-5p levels, and that SPI1 transcriptionally regulated miR-616-5p to promote NSCLC cell invasion and migration. Further analysis showed that SF directly binds SPI1, inhibiting its activity and downregulating miR-616-5p expression, thereby attenuating NSCLC metastasis. However, the poor stability of SF limits its clinical application. To address this, we developed a mesoporous silica nanoparticle (MSN)-based delivery system modified with hyaluronic acid and folic acid (MSNs@SF-HA-FA), which exhibited good stability, enhanced cellular uptake, and improved tumor targeting and anti-metastatic efficacy in NSCLC xenograft models. Collectively, our findings reveal the SPI1/miR-616-5p axis drives NSCLC metastasis and show that MSNs@SF-HA-FA enhances SF's anti-metastatic effect, highlighting its clinical potential.

title_en: Study on transdermal delivery system of sulforaphane chitosan nanoparticles based on methacryloylated chitosan microneedles: application in the treatment of irritant contact dermatitis.
abstract_en: Treatment of irritant contact dermatitis (ICD) is limited by the stratum corneum barrier, which hinders drug delivery and bioavailability. Sulforaphane (SFN) offers anti-inflammatory potential but suffers from poor stability and a short half-life. To address this, we developed a chitosan methacrylate (CSMA) hydrogel microneedle (MN) patch loaded with SFN-chitosan nanoparticles (SFN-CSNPs) for enhanced transdermal delivery.Network pharmacology identified 33 potential ICD-related targets of SFN, including SRC, EGFR, and JAK2, implicating pathways such as AGE-RAGE and Relaxin signaling. SFN-CSNPs were optimized via a Box-Behnken design, yielding nanoparticles with a size of 180-220 nm, PDI < 0.3, a Zeta potential of ∼ 24 mV, and a high encapsulation efficiency (91.94 ± 1.22%). These were incorporated into CSMA MNs using micromolding. The MN arrays exhibited robust mechanical strength (1.53 N/needle), enabling effective stratum corneum penetration. In vitro, the system achieved sustained release (183.6 ± 2.1 µg/cm<sup>2</sup> over 60 h) and 11-fold higher skin retention than the SFN solution. It also demonstrated excellent stability, retaining > 85% drug content after 30 days at 4 °C. In an ICD mouse model, SFN-CSNPs@CSMA MNs significantly reduced erythema, edema, and epidermal thickening (45.37 ± 2.86 µm), approaching normal levels (20.03 ± 3.20 µm), and outperformed both SFN-CSNPs solution and dexamethasone ointment. This nano-MN platform effectively overcomes skin barriers, enhances drug stability, and enables localized, sustained release, offering a promising strategy for delivering unstable natural compounds such as SFN to treat inflammatory skin diseases.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些新的思路，特別是在治療刺激性接觸性皮膚炎（ICD）方面。刺激性接觸性皮膚炎是一種常見的皮膚問題，通常由於外部刺激物引起，導致皮膚發炎和不適。研究中提到的蘿蔔硫素具有抗發炎的潛力，但由於其不穩定性和短暫的半衰期，使用上受到限制。這項研究的創新之處在於開發了一種新型的微針貼片，能夠有效地將蘿蔔硫素傳遞到皮膚深層，這對於改善皮膚健康和預防相關疾病具有重要意義。
para2: 這項研究的主題是針對刺激性接觸性皮膚炎的治療方法，特別是如何提高蘿蔔硫素的傳遞效率。研究者關心的問題是，如何克服皮膚的角質層屏障，以便更有效地將藥物送達到皮膚內部，從而減少炎症反應。研究中使用了網絡藥理學的方法，識別出33個與刺激性接觸性皮膚炎相關的潛在靶點，這些靶點涉及多條信號傳導路徑，顯示出蘿蔔硫素在治療這種皮膚病方面的潛力。
para3: 實驗中，研究者開發了一種含有蘿蔔硫素-幾丁聚糖納米顆粒的幾丁聚糖甲基丙烯酸酯（CSMA）微針貼片。這些納米顆粒的大小在180到220納米之間，並且具有良好的包埋效率。研究者使用微模具技術將這些納米顆粒整合到微針陣列中，這樣的設計使得微針具備了良好的機械強度，能夠有效穿透皮膚的角質層。此外，這個系統在體外實驗中顯示出持續釋放的特性，並且在皮膚上的保留率顯著高於傳統的蘿蔔硫素溶液。
para4: 主要的研究發現是，這種蘿蔔硫素-幾丁聚糖納米顆粒的微針貼片在小鼠模型中顯著減少了皮膚的紅腫、腫脹和表皮增厚，接近正常水平。這表明該系統在治療刺激性接觸性皮膚炎方面具有良好的效果，並且在穩定性方面也表現出色，能夠在低溫下保持超過85%的藥物含量。這項研究提供了一種新的策略，能夠有效克服皮膚屏障，增強藥物的穩定性，並實現局部持續釋放，對於治療這類炎症性皮膚疾病具有潛在的應用價值。

title_en: Adaptive laboratory evolution of Lactobacillus gasseri-01 toward a safe probiotic for enhanced sulforaphane transformation.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane is a potent isothiocyanate with significant physiological activity. Its bioavailability from dietary sources is relatively low and relies on biotransformation by the gut microbiota from glucoraphanin. Lactobacillus gasseri (L. gasseri) is a promising candidate for enhancing sulforaphane transformation because of its probiotic safety and inherent myrosinase-like activity. However, the natural transformation efficiency of the wild-type strain is limited, necessitating enhancement of its transformation capability. This research employed adaptive laboratory evolution (ALE) to enhance the sulforaphane transformation capability of L. gasseri-01 under selective pressure with glucoraphanin as the carbon source. Comprehensive safety assessments and genomic and phenotypic analyses were conducted on the enhanced strain. The results showed that the sulforaphane production rate of the enhanced L. gasseri increased from 2.49 × 10<sup>-4</sup> mg h<sup>-1</sup> logCFU<sup>-1</sup> to 4.52 × 10<sup>-4</sup> mg h<sup>-1</sup> logCFU<sup>-1</sup>. Meanwhile, the enhanced L. gasseri exhibited antibiotic susceptibility to tetracyclines, macrolides and penicillins antibiotics. Importantly, whole genome sequencing showed that the genome of the enhanced strain had been streamlined. Genes related to carbohydrate metabolism, particularly glycoside hydrolase genes, had been enriched, which correlated with the improved utilization rate of glucoraphanin. Consistently, in vivo research confirmed that oral administration of the enhanced L. gasseri could increase the levels of sulforaphane in the cecum and serum, modulate the gut microbiota composition, and increase beneficial bacterial genera such as Akkermansia. Therefore, ALE can generate a functionally enhanced L. gasseri-01 with safety features. This research can provide a viable strategy for enhancing the bioavailability of sulforaphane.
para1: 這項研究對健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，蘿蔔硫素是一種具有顯著生理活性的化合物，對人體健康可能有益。然而，從飲食中獲取的蘿蔔硫素生物利用度相對較低，這意味著我們需要找到方法來提高其吸收率。透過改善腸道微生物的功能，可能能夠增強蘿蔔硫素的轉化效率，進而提升其對健康的正面影響，這對於預防某些疾病或促進健康都有潛在的幫助。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討如何提高腸道益生菌乳酸桿菌（Lactobacillus gasseri）對蘿蔔硫素的轉化能力。研究者關心的是，如何透過改良這種益生菌的特性，來增強其將青花椰菜中的前驅物質（glucoraphanin）轉化為蘿蔔硫素的能力。這不僅涉及到微生物的生物學特性，還包括其在人體內的應用潛力。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用了適應性實驗室進化（ALE）的方法，對乳酸桿菌進行選擇性壓力的培養，以提高其轉化蘿蔔硫素的能力。具體來說，他們以青花椰菜中的前驅物質作為碳源，進行了多次的培養和篩選。此外，研究團隊還對增強後的菌株進行了全面的安全性評估和基因組及表型分析，以確保其安全性和有效性。
para4: 主要的發現是，增強後的乳酸桿菌在轉化蘿蔔硫素的效率上有顯著提升，生產速率從原本的每小時每百萬菌落數2.49×10^-4毫克提高到4.52×10^-4毫克。同時，這種增強菌株對某些抗生素仍然保持敏感，顯示出其安全性。此外，基因組測序結果顯示，增強菌株的基因組已經被精簡，與碳水化合物代謝相關的基因得到了豐富，這與其對青花椰菜前驅物的利用率提升有關。進一步的研究也確認，口服增強的乳酸桿菌能提高腸道和血清中的蘿蔔硫素水平，並調節腸道微生物組的組成，增加有益細菌的數量。
explanation_zh:
這項研究對健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，蘿蔔硫素是一種具有顯著生理活性的化合物，對人體健康可能有益。然而，從飲食中獲取的蘿蔔硫素生物利用度相對較低，這意味著我們需要找到方法來提高其吸收率。透過改善腸道微生物的功能，可能能夠增強蘿蔔硫素的轉化效率，進而提升其對健康的正面影響，這對於預防某些疾病或促進健康都有潛在的幫助。

這項研究的主題是探討如何提高腸道益生菌乳酸桿菌（Lactobacillus gasseri）對蘿蔔硫素的轉化能力。研究者關心的是，如何透過改良這種益生菌的特性，來增強其將青花椰菜中的前驅物質（glucoraphanin）轉化為蘿蔔硫素的能力。這不僅涉及到微生物的生物學特性，還包括其在人體內的應用潛力。

實驗中，研究者使用了適應性實驗室進化（ALE）的方法，對乳酸桿菌進行選擇性壓力的培養，以提高其轉化蘿蔔硫素的能力。具體來說，他們以青花椰菜中的前驅物質作為碳源，進行了多次的培養和篩選。此外，研究團隊還對增強後的菌株進行了全面的安全性評估和基因組及表型分析，以確保其安全性和有效性。

主要的發現是，增強後的乳酸桿菌在轉化蘿蔔硫素的效率上有顯著提升，生產速率從原本的每小時每百萬菌落數2.49×10^-4毫克提高到4.52×10^-4毫克。同時，這種增強菌株對某些抗生素仍然保持敏感，顯示出其安全性。此外，基因組測序結果顯示，增強菌株的基因組已經被精簡，與碳水化合物代謝相關的基因得到了豐富，這與其對青花椰菜前驅物的利用率提升有關。進一步的研究也確認，口服增強的乳酸桿菌能提高腸道和血清中的蘿蔔硫素水平，並調節腸道微生物組的組成，增加有益細菌的數量。

3. 增強的乳酸桿菌在安全性和有效性上都有良好的表現。

title_en: Sulforaphane improved cognitive behavior in APP/PS1 mice via promoting structural and functional synaptic plasticity in the hippocampus.
abstract_en: Cognitive dysfunction is the main clinical feature of Alzheimer's disease (AD), and oxidative stress is considered a critical contributor to AD pathogenesis. Sulforaphane (SFN), an aliphatic isothiocyanate predominantly derived from cruciferous vegetables, has been reported to exert antioxidant and neuroprotective effects; however, its impact on synaptic plasticity and the underlying electrophysiological mechanisms in AD remain unclear. In this study, ten-month-old male APPswe/PS1dE9 double transgenic (APP/PS1) mice and wild-type (WT) littermates were randomized to four groups (WT + saline, WT + SFN, APP/PS1 + saline, and APP/PS1 + SFN). SFN was administered at 10 mg kg<sup>-1</sup>via intraperitoneal injection for 30 d in vivo and 1 μM concentration in vitro. Then, we investigated whether SFN improves cognition by restoring hippocampal synaptic plasticity in APP/PS1 mice. It was suggested that SFN administration significantly attenuated Aβ₁₋₄₂-induced oxidative damage in vitro and improved spatial learning and reference memory deficits in APP/PS1 mice. Bioinformatics analysis suggested that SFN modulated synapse-related pathways associated with AD. Consistent with these findings, electrophysiological recordings demonstrated that SFN alleviated hippocampal long-term potentiation (LTP) impairment. Moreover, SFN increased the expression of synaptic proteins PSD-95 and synaptophysin and enhanced dendritic complexity and dendritic spine density in the hippocampal CA1 region, as assessed by immunoblotting and Golgi staining. Together, these results indicated that SFN ameliorated cognitive deficits in AD mice by alleviating LTP inhibition and restoring synaptic structural integrity, supporting a role for synaptic plasticity in the neuroprotective effects of SFN.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些關於健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是針對阿茲罕默症這種影響認知功能的疾病。研究指出，氧化壓力可能是導致阿茲罕默症發展的重要因素，而蘿蔔硫素作為一種來自十字花科蔬菜的化合物，可能有助於減少這種氧化壓力，進而改善認知功能。這意味著，透過飲食中增加這類蔬菜的攝取，可能對於預防或減緩阿茲罕默症的進展有潛在的好處。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對阿茲罕默症小鼠的認知功能和突觸可塑性的影響。研究者關心的問題是，蘿蔔硫素是否能改善阿茲罕默症小鼠的認知缺損，並且了解其背後的電生理機制。這項研究希望能揭示蘿蔔硫素在神經保護方面的潛力，特別是在改善突觸結構和功能方面的作用。
para3: 實驗中使用了十個月大的APPswe/PS1dE9雙轉基因小鼠和野生型小鼠，並將它們隨機分為四組：野生型加生理鹽水、野生型加蘿蔔硫素、阿茲罕默症小鼠加生理鹽水、以及阿茲罕默症小鼠加蘿蔔硫素。蘿蔔硫素以每公斤10毫克的劑量通過腹腔注射給予小鼠，持續30天，並在體外以1微摩爾的濃度進行實驗。研究者接著檢測蘿蔔硫素是否能改善阿茲罕默症小鼠的海馬突觸可塑性。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素的給予顯著減少了由Aβ₁₋₄₂引起的氧化損傷，並改善了阿茲罕默症小鼠的空間學習和記憶缺損。此外，生物信息學分析顯示，蘿蔔硫素調節了與阿茲罕默症相關的突觸通路。電生理記錄也顯示，蘿蔔硫素減輕了海馬長期增強（LTP）的損害，並增加了突觸蛋白的表達，改善了海馬CA1區的樹突複雜性和樹突棘密度。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過改善突觸結構的完整性來減輕阿茲罕默症小鼠的認知缺損。

title_en: Dietary sulforaphane glucosinolate alleviates stress-induced depression and demyelination through gut-brain axis modulation.
abstract_en: Chronic stress is a major risk factor for depression and white matter injury, yet effective preventive strategies remain limited. Here, we investigated whether an 8-week diet supplemented with 0.1% sulforaphane glucosinolate (SGS) could prevent depression-like behaviors and corpus callosum (CC) demyelination in C57BL/6 mice exposed to 14 d of chronic restraint stress (CRS). SGS pre-treatment significantly reduced immobility in the forced swim test and showed a trend toward preserving sucrose preference compared with stressed controls. Consistently, Black-Gold II staining and myelin basic protein (MBP) immunofluorescence demonstrated that SGS markedly attenuated CRS-induced myelin loss in the corpus callosum. Gut microbiota profiling by 16S rRNA sequencing revealed unchanged α-diversity but clear β-diversity shifts, including enrichment of taxa such as Lacrimispora and Roseburia in SGS-fed mice under CRS. Untargeted plasma metabolomics further showed that SGS normalized stress-elevated metabolites, and correlation analyses linked specific microbial taxa and metabolites to myelin integrity. Together, these findings indicate that dietary SGS confers stress resilience and preserves white matter integrity, at least in part, through modulation of the gut-brain axis. SGS therefore represents a promising nutraceutical strategy for the prevention of stress-related neuropsychiatric and white matter disorders.
para1: 慢性壓力是導致憂鬱症和白質損傷的重要風險因素，但目前有效的預防策略仍然有限。這項研究探討了飲食中添加蘿蔔硫素是否能夠幫助預防與壓力相關的情緒問題和神經損傷，這對於改善心理健康和預防相關疾病具有潛在的啟示。如果能透過飲食調整來增強對壓力的抵抗力，將有助於減少憂鬱症等心理健康問題的發生，並且可能對維持大腦健康有正面影響。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在慢性壓力情境下對小鼠的影響，特別是它是否能夠預防憂鬱症狀和白質的脫髓鞘現象。研究者關心的是在長期壓力下，這種天然化合物是否能改善小鼠的行為表現，並保護其神經系統的完整性，這對於理解壓力對大腦的影響及其潛在的預防方法非常重要。
para3: 實驗中使用了C57BL/6小鼠，這些小鼠在經歷了14天的慢性約束壓力後，接受了為期8週的蘿蔔硫素飲食補充。研究者透過強迫游泳測試來評估小鼠的行為變化，並使用染色技術和免疫螢光來檢測小鼠大腦中的髓鞘損失情況。此外，還進行了腸道微生物群的分析和血漿代謝物的檢測，以了解蘿蔔硫素對小鼠生理狀態的影響。
para4: 研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素的補充能顯著減少小鼠在強迫游泳測試中的靜止時間，並有助於維持其對糖的偏好，這表明其可能改善了情緒狀態。此外，蘿蔔硫素還顯著減少了由慢性壓力引起的髓鞘損失。腸道微生物分析顯示，蘿蔔硫素飲食改變了小鼠的微生物組成，並且與髓鞘的完整性有關聯。這些發現表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過調節腸腦軸來增強對壓力的抵抗力，並保護白質的完整性，顯示出其作為營養補充劑在預防壓力相關神經精神疾病方面的潛力。

title_en: Sulforaphane preserves hepatocellular metabolic regulation and organelle integrity in a model of diet-induced obesity.
abstract_en: Metabolic-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) is strongly associated with obesity and type 2 diabetes (T2D), emerging as a consequence of sustained disturbances in hepatocellular metabolic signaling, inflammatory regulation, and organelle homeostasis induced by nutritional overload. Sulforaphane (SFN), a dietary bioactive compound, has been associated with metabolic protection, yet its ability to preserve hepatic cellular regulation and ultrastructural integrity during MASLD development remains incompletely defined. In this study, C57BL/6 mice were exposed to a high-fat, high-sucrose diet for 14 weeks to induce MASLD, with concomitant SFN administration (2 mg/kg/day, intraperitoneally, 5 days/week). Hepatic and adipose tissue remodeling, insulin signaling, inflammatory pathways, and subcellular architecture were evaluated using biochemical analyses, histology, gene expression profiling, and transmission electron microscopy. SFN markedly attenuated hepatic steatosis, collagen deposition, and glycogen degeneration, while modulating insulin signaling pathway and shifting hepatic gene expression toward oxidative metabolic pathways. These effects were accompanied by suppression of inflammatory mediators, including Nlrp3, Tnfa, and Il1β. At the ultrastructural level, SFN preserved mitochondrial architecture and endoplasmic reticulum organization, preventing diet-induced organelle stress, lipid overload and energy imbalance. Collectively, these findings demonstrate that SFN protects against diet-induced MASLD by maintaining hepatocellular metabolic and inflammatory regulation and preserving organelle integrity, highlighting the relevance of cellular regulatory mechanisms in the control of hepatic intermediary metabolism.
para1: 這項研究對健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，隨著肥胖和第二型糖尿病的增加，代謝相關的脂肪肝疾病（MASLD）也逐漸成為一個重要的健康問題。這種疾病的發展與我們的飲食習慣密切相關，特別是高脂肪和高糖的飲食。研究顯示，蘿蔔硫素這種天然化合物可能在保護肝臟健康方面發揮重要作用，這對於預防和管理MASLD具有潛在的意義。透過改善肝臟的代謝和炎症調控，蘿蔔硫素可能成為未來飲食干預的一個重要成分，幫助人們維持健康的肝臟功能，降低相關疾病的風險。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在代謝相關脂肪肝疾病（MASLD）發展過程中的作用。研究者關心的是，當肝臟受到高脂肪和高糖飲食的影響時，蘿蔔硫素是否能夠幫助維持肝細胞的正常功能和結構。由於MASLD與肥胖和第二型糖尿病有著密切的關聯，因此了解蘿蔔硫素的潛在保護作用，對於改善這些疾病的管理和預防至關重要。
para3: 在這項研究中，研究人員使用C57BL/6小鼠作為實驗對象，讓它們在14週內接受高脂肪和高糖的飲食，以誘導MASLD的發展。同時，這些小鼠每天接受2毫克的蘿蔔硫素注射，持續五天一週。研究團隊透過生化分析、組織學、基因表達分析和透射電子顯微鏡等方法，評估肝臟和脂肪組織的重塑、胰島素信號傳導、炎症途徑以及細胞內結構的變化。
para4: 主要的研究發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素能顯著減少肝臟脂肪變性、膠原沉積和糖原退化，並調節胰島素信號傳導路徑，促使肝臟基因表達朝向氧化代謝途徑轉變。此外，蘿蔔硫素還能抑制一些炎症介質的表現，如Nlrp3、Tnfa和Il1β。在超微結構層面上，蘿蔔硫素能夠保護線粒體的結構和內質網的組織，防止因飲食引起的細胞器壓力、脂質過載和能量失衡。這些結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素在飲食誘導的MASLD中具有保護作用，強調了細胞調控機制在肝臟代謝中的重要性。

title_en: Sulforaphane reprograms the metabolomic landscape of chronic kidney disease: insights from NMR-based metabolomics.
abstract_en: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is characterized by systemic metabolic disturbances and oxidative stress. Nutritional interventions using bioactive compounds such as sulforaphane (SFN) may represent a promising adjuvant strategy to attenuate these alterations. This study evaluated the effects of SFN supplementation on the untargeted metabolomic profile of non-dialysis CKD patients. A randomized, placebo-controlled clinical trial was conducted in 14 patients with CKD stages 3-5 who received either 400 µg per day of SFN or placebo for 30 days. Serum samples were analyzed using proton nuclear magnetic resonance (<sup>1</sup>H NMR) spectroscopy to identify metabolomic alterations and their correlations with markers of oxidative stress, inflammation, and gene expression. SFN supplementation tended to reduce 2-hydroxyisovalerate and glycerol levels, metabolites associated with insulin resistance and oxidative stress, and attenuated methanol elevation. Correlations were identified between heme oxygenase-1 (HO-1) and several amino acids, and between NRF2 and glycerol, suggesting an interplay between amino acid metabolism and antioxidant defense. Overall, SFN modulated the metabolomic profile of CKD patients, particularly metabolites related to mitochondrial function, lipid metabolism, and oxidative balance. These findings demonstrate that NMR-based metabolomics is a powerful non-invasive approach for elucidating the molecular effects of nutritional therapies in CKD and support SFN as a potential nutraceutical strategy in renal care.
para1: 慢性腎臟病（CKD）是一種影響全身代謝和氧化壓力的疾病。這項研究顯示，透過營養介入，特別是使用像蘿蔔硫素這樣的生物活性化合物，可能有助於改善這些代謝異常。這意味著，透過飲食調整，或許能在某種程度上減輕慢性腎臟病患者的症狀，並促進他們的健康，這對於疾病的預防和管理都具有重要的啟示。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素補充對非透析慢性腎臟病患者的代謝組學影響。研究者關心的是，這種營養補充劑是否能改善患者的代謝狀況，並減少與氧化壓力和發炎相關的生化指標。透過這項研究，科學家希望能更深入了解蘿蔔硫素在慢性腎臟病管理中的潛在作用。
para3: 這項研究採用隨機、安慰劑對照的臨床試驗設計，參與者為14名慢性腎臟病第3至第5期的患者。這些患者在30天內接受每日400微克的蘿蔔硫素或安慰劑。研究團隊利用質子核磁共振（<sup>1</sup>H NMR）光譜技術分析血清樣本，以識別代謝組學的變化，並探討這些變化與氧化壓力、發炎和基因表達的關聯。
para4: 研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素補充可能有助於降低與胰島素抵抗和氧化壓力相關的代謝物質水平，如2-羥基異戊酸和甘油，並減少甲醇的升高。此外，研究還發現了血紅素氧化酶-1（HO-1）與幾種氨基酸之間的相關性，以及NRF2與甘油之間的關聯，這表明氨基酸代謝與抗氧化防禦之間存在相互作用。總體而言，蘿蔔硫素調節了慢性腎臟病患者的代謝組學特徵，尤其是與線粒體功能、脂質代謝和氧化平衡相關的代謝物，這些發現支持了蘿蔔硫素作為腎臟護理潛在營養策略的可能性。
explanation_zh:
慢性腎臟病（CKD）是一種影響全身代謝和氧化壓力的疾病。這項研究顯示，透過營養介入，特別是使用像蘿蔔硫素這樣的生物活性化合物，可能有助於改善這些代謝異常。這意味著，透過飲食調整，或許能在某種程度上減輕慢性腎臟病患者的症狀，並促進他們的健康，這對於疾病的預防和管理都具有重要的啟示。

本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素補充對非透析慢性腎臟病患者的代謝組學影響。研究者關心的是，這種營養補充劑是否能改善患者的代謝狀況，並減少與氧化壓力和發炎相關的生化指標。透過這項研究，科學家希望能更深入了解蘿蔔硫素在慢性腎臟病管理中的潛在作用。

這項研究採用隨機、安慰劑對照的臨床試驗設計，參與者為14名慢性腎臟病第3至第5期的患者。這些患者在30天內接受每日400微克的蘿蔔硫素或安慰劑。研究團隊利用質子核磁共振（<sup>1</sup>H NMR）光譜技術分析血清樣本，以識別代謝組學的變化，並探討這些變化與氧化壓力、發炎和基因表達的關聯。

研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素補充可能有助於降低與胰島素抵抗和氧化壓力相關的代謝物質水平，如2-羥基異戊酸和甘油，並減少甲醇的升高。此外，研究還發現了血紅素氧化酶-1（HO-1）與幾種氨基酸之間的相關性，以及NRF2與甘油之間的關聯，這表明氨基酸代謝與抗氧化防禦之間存在相互作用。總體而言，蘿蔔硫素調節了慢性腎臟病患者的代謝組學特徵，尤其是與線粒體功能、脂質代謝和氧化平衡相關的代謝物，這些發現支持了蘿蔔硫素作為腎臟護理潛在營養策略的可能性。

title_en: Prevention of Myc-Driven Prostate Cancer in Mice by Oral Administration of Sulforaphane.
abstract_en: Prevention is desirable to reduce suffering and death from prostate cancer. However, a clinical-grade intervention for the prevention of this malignancy is still lacking. This study was undertaken to determine the feasibility of prostate cancer prevention by broccoli constituent sulforaphane (SFN) because epidemiological studies have suggested an inverse association between intake of broccoli and the risk of prostate cancer. Oral administration of 1 mg SFN/mouse (three times/week) to 5-week-old male Hi-Myc transgenic mice for 5 weeks decreased the incidence of prostatic adenocarcinoma in situ by about 54% without causing weight loss or any other side effects. Existing literature strongly implicates fatty acid synthesis in prostate cancer progression. Therefore, we determined the effect of SFN treatment on fatty acid synthesis. Prostate cancer prevention by SFN in Hi-Myc mice was accompanied by a decrease in: (a) the uptake of <sup>11</sup>C-acetate in the prostate of live mice; (b) plasma levels of fatty acid synthesis intermediates acetyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA; (c) circulating levels of total free fatty acids (TFFA); (d) downregulation of fatty acid synthesis enzyme proteins acetyl-CoA carboxylase 1 and fatty acid synthase; and (e) plasma levels of interkeukin-10 and interleukin-12. Because prostate cancer in Hi-Myc mice is driven by Myc, we determined the effect of overexpression of c-Myc in 22Rv1 cells on TFFA level. Overexpression of c-Myc conferred partial protection against TFFA suppression by SFN treatment. In summary, this study reveals that SFN administration prevents prostate cancer development in Hi-Myc mice by suppressing fatty acid synthesis.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在前列腺癌的預防方面。前列腺癌是一種常見的男性癌症，能夠找到有效的預防方法將有助於減少患者的痛苦和死亡率。雖然目前尚未有臨床級的介入措施來預防這種癌症，但研究顯示，青花椰菜中的一種成分——蘿蔔硫素，可能對降低前列腺癌的風險有幫助。這讓我們對飲食中的某些成分在癌症預防中的潛力有了新的認識，未來可能會有更多的研究來探索這方面的可能性。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在前列腺癌預防中的可行性。研究人員關注的問題是，青花椰菜的攝取是否與前列腺癌的風險有關，並希望通過實驗來驗證蘿蔔硫素的效果。過去的流行病學研究已經暗示，攝取青花椰菜可能與前列腺癌風險呈負相關，因此這項研究旨在進一步了解這種關聯的機制及其潛在的預防效果。
para3: 在實驗中，研究人員使用了5週大的雄性Hi-Myc轉基因小鼠作為實驗對象，這些小鼠被用來模擬前列腺癌的發展。研究方法是每週三次口服給予小鼠1毫克的蘿蔔硫素，持續5週。研究團隊觀察了蘿蔔硫素對小鼠前列腺腺癌的影響，並評估了其對脂肪酸合成的作用。這樣的設計使得研究能夠探討蘿蔔硫素在癌症預防中的潛在機制，並評估其安全性和有效性。
para4: 主要的發現是，蘿蔔硫素的給予能夠顯著降低小鼠前列腺腺癌的發生率，約減少了54%。此外，研究還發現，蘿蔔硫素的治療伴隨著脂肪酸合成的抑制，包括前列腺對於<sup>11</sup>C-醋酸的攝取減少、脂肪酸合成中間體的血漿水平下降，以及相關酶的表達下調。這些結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素可能通過抑制脂肪酸合成來防止前列腺癌的發展，儘管這些結果仍需進一步的研究來確認其在臨床上的應用潛力。
explanation_zh:
這項研究對於健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在前列腺癌的預防方面。前列腺癌是一種常見的男性癌症，能夠找到有效的預防方法將有助於減少患者的痛苦和死亡率。雖然目前尚未有臨床級的介入措施來預防這種癌症，但研究顯示，青花椰菜中的一種成分——蘿蔔硫素，可能對降低前列腺癌的風險有幫助。這讓我們對飲食中的某些成分在癌症預防中的潛力有了新的認識，未來可能會有更多的研究來探索這方面的可能性。

這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素在前列腺癌預防中的可行性。研究人員關注的問題是，青花椰菜的攝取是否與前列腺癌的風險有關，並希望通過實驗來驗證蘿蔔硫素的效果。過去的流行病學研究已經暗示，攝取青花椰菜可能與前列腺癌風險呈負相關，因此這項研究旨在進一步了解這種關聯的機制及其潛在的預防效果。

在實驗中，研究人員使用了5週大的雄性Hi-Myc轉基因小鼠作為實驗對象，這些小鼠被用來模擬前列腺癌的發展。研究方法是每週三次口服給予小鼠1毫克的蘿蔔硫素，持續5週。研究團隊觀察了蘿蔔硫素對小鼠前列腺腺癌的影響，並評估了其對脂肪酸合成的作用。這樣的設計使得研究能夠探討蘿蔔硫素在癌症預防中的潛在機制，並評估其安全性和有效性。

主要的發現是，蘿蔔硫素的給予能夠顯著降低小鼠前列腺腺癌的發生率，約減少了54%。此外，研究還發現，蘿蔔硫素的治療伴隨著脂肪酸合成的抑制，包括前列腺對於<sup>11</sup>C-醋酸的攝取減少、脂肪酸合成中間體的血漿水平下降，以及相關酶的表達下調。這些結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素可能通過抑制脂肪酸合成來防止前列腺癌的發展，儘管這些結果仍需進一步的研究來確認其在臨床上的應用潛力。

title_en: Sulforaphane Microcapsules via O/W Emulsion: Development, Characterization, and Application in Functional Yogurt.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SFN) is an anti-cancer isothiocyanate occurring in Brassicaceae. SFN is decomposed by heat, oxygen, and alkaline conditions. Microencapsulation is a way to improve its stability. This work presents the development of SFN microcapsules using an oil-in-water emulsion with gum arabic (GA) as the wall material for incorporation into yogurt. The process for obtaining SFN microcapsules was optimized using response surface methodology. The optimal microencapsulation conditions were 7 min of stirring, an SFN/GA mass ratio of 0.7, and a surfactant concentration of 7%, resulting in an entrapment efficiency of 90.0 ± 3.0%, the highest reported. The microcapsules had a regular spherical shape with 0.5-5.5 µm diameter and no cracks. Freezing temperature (-4 °C) preserved 100% of SFN in the microcapsule for 90 days. Yogurt added with SFN microcapsules maintained physical and microbiological quality. SFN retention in yogurt after 30 days was 57% when microencapsulated, showing a 14-fold reduction in the kinetic degradation constant compared with free SFN, confirming the efficacy of this method.
para1: 這項研究對於健康和疾病預防有著重要的啟示，特別是在抗癌方面。蘿蔔硫素是一種存在於十字花科植物中的化合物，具有抗癌特性。然而，這種化合物在高溫、氧氣和鹼性環境下容易分解，這使得它的應用受到限制。透過微膠囊技術，可以提高蘿蔔硫素的穩定性，進而可能增強其在食品中的健康效益，特別是在發酵乳製品如優格中，這對於希望透過飲食來增進健康的人來說，提供了一個新的選擇。
para2: 本研究的主題是開發一種新的微膠囊技術，以提高蘿蔔硫素的穩定性，並將其應用於優格中。研究者關心的問題是如何有效地包裹蘿蔔硫素，以防止其在儲存和加工過程中分解，並保持其抗癌特性。這項研究不僅探討了微膠囊的製作過程，還評估了其在優格中的表現，這對於食品科學和營養學的研究具有重要意義。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用油包水的乳液技術，並以阿拉伯膠作為包覆材料來製作蘿蔔硫素微膠囊。研究者透過反應曲面法來優化微膠囊的製作條件，最終確定了最佳的攪拌時間為7分鐘，蘿蔔硫素與阿拉伯膠的質量比為0.7，表面活性劑的濃度為7%。這些條件下，微膠囊的包埋效率達到90%，顯示出良好的製作效果。微膠囊的直徑在0.5至5.5微米之間，形狀規則且無裂縫，這些特徵使其適合用於食品中。
para4: 主要的發現是，經過微膠囊包覆的蘿蔔硫素在優格中能夠保持其穩定性，並在冷凍條件下能夠保存100%的蘿蔔硫素達90天。在優格中添加微膠囊後，經過30天的儲存，蘿蔔硫素的保留率為57%，這顯示出微膠囊技術能顯著減少蘿蔔硫素的降解速率，與未包覆的蘿蔔硫素相比，降解常數降低了14倍，這證實了這種方法的有效性。
explanation_zh:
這項研究對於健康和疾病預防有著重要的啟示，特別是在抗癌方面。蘿蔔硫素是一種存在於十字花科植物中的化合物，具有抗癌特性。然而，這種化合物在高溫、氧氣和鹼性環境下容易分解，這使得它的應用受到限制。透過微膠囊技術，可以提高蘿蔔硫素的穩定性，進而可能增強其在食品中的健康效益，特別是在發酵乳製品如優格中，這對於希望透過飲食來增進健康的人來說，提供了一個新的選擇。

本研究的主題是開發一種新的微膠囊技術，以提高蘿蔔硫素的穩定性，並將其應用於優格中。研究者關心的問題是如何有效地包裹蘿蔔硫素，以防止其在儲存和加工過程中分解，並保持其抗癌特性。這項研究不僅探討了微膠囊的製作過程，還評估了其在優格中的表現，這對於食品科學和營養學的研究具有重要意義。

實驗中，研究者使用油包水的乳液技術，並以阿拉伯膠作為包覆材料來製作蘿蔔硫素微膠囊。研究者透過反應曲面法來優化微膠囊的製作條件，最終確定了最佳的攪拌時間為7分鐘，蘿蔔硫素與阿拉伯膠的質量比為0.7，表面活性劑的濃度為7%。這些條件下，微膠囊的包埋效率達到90%，顯示出良好的製作效果。微膠囊的直徑在0.5至5.5微米之間，形狀規則且無裂縫，這些特徵使其適合用於食品中。

主要的發現是，經過微膠囊包覆的蘿蔔硫素在優格中能夠保持其穩定性，並在冷凍條件下能夠保存100%的蘿蔔硫素達90天。在優格中添加微膠囊後，經過30天的儲存，蘿蔔硫素的保留率為57%，這顯示出微膠囊技術能顯著減少蘿蔔硫素的降解速率，與未包覆的蘿蔔硫素相比，降解常數降低了14倍，這證實了這種方法的有效性。

title_en: The H<sub>2</sub>S donor sulforaphane inhibits NLRP<sub>3</sub> inflammasome activation by inducing mitochondrial autophagy and mitigating CBS-H<sub>2</sub>S axis damage in in-vitro and in-vivo models of Parkinson's disease.
abstract_en: Hydrogen sulfide (H₂S) plays a crucial neuroprotective role in Parkinson's disease (PD). Cystathionine-β-synthase (CBS), a key enzyme involved in H₂S biosynthesis, exhibits expression deficiencies that are closely linked to PD progression. This suggests that enhancing the CBS-H₂S signaling axis to restore H₂S homeostasis may be a critical approach for preventing and treating PD. In the present study, the H₂S donor sulforaphane (SFN) was investigated to elucidate its neuroprotective mechanisms through activation of the CBS-H₂S axis. siRNA-mediated silencing of Nrf2 and CBS was employed to clarify the role of each in SFN's effects. Our findings demonstrate that SFN promotes CBS expression and H₂S synthesis, activates mitophagy to clear damaged mitochondria, reduces mitochondrial-derived reactive oxygen species (mtROS) levels, and inhibits the activation of NLRP3 inflammasomes and caspase-1. In the MPTP-induced PD mouse model, SFN improved motor performance, increased the survival rate of tyrosine hydroxylase (TH)-positive dopaminergic neurons in the substantia nigra, and restored dopamine metabolism in the striatum, normalizing the DOPAC/DA and 5-HIAA/5-HT ratios. Electron microscopy revealed that SFN facilitated the clearance of damaged mitochondria through autophagosomes and blocked mtROS-mediated NLRP3 inflammasome activation. In the MPP<sup>+</sup>-induced BV-2 microglial cell model, SFN upregulated CBS expression, enhanced H₂S synthesis, increased the LC3-II/I ratio, and inhibited p62 degradation, thereby promoting the recovery of mitochondrial membrane potential and reducing ROS release. These effects were still observed under Nrf2 silencing conditions, indicating that SFN's neuroprotective effects are mediated through the CBS-H₂S axis independently of Nrf2 signaling. Collectively, these findings indicate that SFN reshapes the CBS-H₂S signaling axis, activates mitochondrial autophagy, and suppresses inflammation, offering novel insights into multi-target therapeutic approaches for PD and underscoring the essential role of H₂S in neuroprotection.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些對健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是在帕金森病的預防和治療方面。研究指出，氫硫化物（H₂S）在神經保護中扮演著重要角色，這意味著透過調整體內的H₂S水平，可能有助於減緩或預防帕金森病的進展。這樣的發現讓我們思考，如何利用天然物質來改善神經系統的健康，並可能成為未來治療帕金森病的新方向。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素（SFN）在帕金森病中的神經保護機制。研究者關注的是如何透過增強CBS-H₂S信號通路來改善H₂S的穩態，進而對抗帕金森病的進展。這項研究特別強調了CBS這個關鍵酶在H₂S合成中的角色，並試圖揭示其在疾病進程中的重要性。

para4: 主要發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素能促進CBS的表達和H₂S的合成，並激活線粒體自噬，清除受損的線粒體。此外，研究還發現蘿蔔硫素能降低線粒體衍生的活性氧（mtROS）水平，並抑制NLRP3炎症小體和caspase-1的活化。在帕金森病小鼠模型中，蘿蔔硫素改善了運動表現，增加了多巴胺能神經元的存活率，並恢復了多巴胺的代謝。這些結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素可能透過多重機制來提供神經保護，但仍需進一步研究來確認其具體效果。

title_en: Sulforaphane as a Photoprotective Agent Against UV-Induced Skin Damage and Carcinogenesis: A Scoping Review.
abstract_en: Background/Objectives: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation is a major environmental carcinogen responsible for skin damage through oxidative stress, DNA damage, and inflammation. The nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) pathway plays a central role in regulating cellular antioxidant defences against UV-induced damage. This scoping review aims to evaluate the potential role of sulforaphane (SFN), a known Nrf2 inducer, in protecting against UV-induced skin damage and photocarcinogenesis. Methods: A literature search was conducted in PubMed and Scopus from inception to 27 January 2026, to identify original experimental studies investigating SFN, glucoraphanin, or broccoli sprout extracts in the context of UV-induced skin damage. Eligible studies included in vitro, ex vivo, in vivo, and human models assessing outcomes related to oxidative stress, inflammation, molecular signalling pathways, and tumour development. Following screening and eligibility assessment, twelve studies were included in the qualitative synthesis. Results: The included studies suggest that SFN exerts photoprotective effects across multiple experimental models. In murine studies, SFN and SFN-rich extracts were associated with a reduction in tumour incidence, multiplicity, and volume following UV exposure. In human studies, topical SFN application reduced UV-induced erythema and induced cytoprotective enzyme expression, although clinical evidence remains limited. Mechanistically, SFN consistently activated the Nrf2 pathway, leading to increased expression of antioxidant and phase II detoxifying enzymes, and was associated with modulation of inflammatory responses and inhibition of MAPK/AP-1 signalling. Emerging evidence also indicates potential effects on UV-induced metabolic and epigenetic alterations. Conclusions: Current evidence supports a potential role for sulforaphane in mitigating UV-induced skin damage through activation of endogenous defence pathways. However, the available data are predominantly preclinical, and further well-designed clinical studies are needed to clarify its efficacy and translational relevance in humans.

title_en: BolMYB122-BolTGG1 module enhances salt stress tolerance via accelerating the hydrolysis of glucosinolates to produce sulforaphane in broccoli.
abstract_en: Glucosinolates (GSLs), one type of secondary metabolites mainly enriched in cruciferous plants, play important roles in environmental stress responses. However, the function and regulatory mechanism of GSLs in combating salt stress in broccoli are largely unclear. In this study, BolTGG1, a member of β-thioglucoside glucohydrolase (TGG) genes mainly functioning to hydrolyze GSLs, was identified in broccoli. BolTGG1 expression was upregulated under salt stress. Overexpression of BolTGG1 significantly improved the tolerance to salt stress in both broccoli and Arabidopsis. Subsequently, BolTGG1 was heterologously expressed in yeast and the BolTGG1 protein was isolated to evaluate its ability to hydrolyze GSLs. The results confirmed that exogenous addition of BolTGG1 could obviously increase the yield of sulforaphane (SFN), a major hydrolysis product of GSLs, in vitro. Meanwhile, the content of SFN was significantly increased in BolTGG1-overexpression transgenic broccoli plants. The role of SFN in the salt stress response was then explored. The results indicated that external application of SFN could directly enhance the salt resistance with the elevated activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD) and peroxidase (POD), and reduced malondialdehyde (MDA) levels in broccoli. Furthermore, BolMYB122 was confirmed to be the upstream regulatory transcription factor of BolTGG1. BolMYB122 positively regulated BolTGG1 transcription. In summary, these results indicated that BolMYB122-BolTGG1 module confers salt stress tolerance mainly by accelerating the generation of SFN. These findings provide new insights into the role and regulatory mechanism of GSL metabolites in plant salt stress response, and suggest a novel breeding strategy for stress resistance from the perspective of regulating plant secondary metabolism.
para1: 這項研究提供了一些有關健康和疾病預防的啟示，特別是在植物如何應對環境壓力方面。青花椰菜等十字花科植物中的一種次級代謝物——蘿蔔硫素，可能在抵抗鹽分壓力中扮演重要角色。這不僅對農業生產有幫助，還可能對人類健康有潛在的影響，因為這些植物的抗壓能力可能與其營養價值有關。了解這些機制或許能幫助我們在未來開發出更具抗逆性的作物，進而促進人類的健康與福祉。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討青花椰菜中的一種基因BolTGG1在鹽分壓力下的表現及其功能。研究者關心的是這個基因如何影響青花椰菜對鹽分的耐受性，以及它在蘿蔔硫素生成中的角色。透過這項研究，科學家希望能夠揭示青花椰菜在面對環境挑戰時的生理反應，並進一步了解次級代謝物在植物應對壓力中的重要性。
para3: 實驗中，研究者首先確認了BolTGG1基因在青花椰菜中的表現，並發現其在鹽分壓力下會上調。接著，研究者在青花椰菜和阿拉伯芥中過度表達這個基因，以評估其對鹽分耐受性的影響。此外，BolTGG1蛋白在酵母中被異源表達，並進行了酶活性測試，以確認其對蘿蔔硫素的水解能力。這些方法幫助研究者深入了解BolTGG1的功能及其在鹽分壓力下的作用。
para4: 主要發現顯示，過度表達BolTGG1的青花椰菜在鹽分壓力下顯示出更強的耐受性，並且在體外實驗中，添加BolTGG1能顯著提高蘿蔔硫素的產量。此外，外部施加蘿蔔硫素能直接增強青花椰菜的鹽分抵抗力，並提高超氧化物歧化酶和過氧化物酶的活性，同時降低了丙二醛的含量。這些結果表明，BolMYB122基因是BolTGG1的上游調控因子，並且正向調控BolTGG1的表達，這一模組可能是青花椰菜抵抗鹽分壓力的重要機制。

title_en: CHG Hypomethylation of BoCYP83A1 May Contribute to Methyl Jasmonate-Induced Sulforaphane Accumulation in Broccoli.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SFN) is widely recognized for its health-promoting benefits. While several studies have reported that methyl jasmonate (MeJA) enhances SFN accumulation, the role of epigenetic regulation remains unclear. Here, we performed an integrated analysis of transcriptome, whole-genome bisulfite sequencing, and small RNA-seq data from broccoli florets and leaves under MeJA treatment and control conditions. Our results revealed that MeJA-responsive differentially methylated regions (DMRs) predominantly occurring in the CHG context. Integrative analysis of methylation and siRNA data indicated that changes in MeJA-responsive CHH methylation were associated with 24-nucleotide siRNA clusters. Furthermore, inhibiting DNA methylation using 5-azacytidine treatment in broccoli seedlings increased SFN content, validating the role of DNA hypomethylation in this process. By combining transcriptional regulatory network analysis with DMR data, we identified the MeJA-responsive CHG-hypomethylated gene BoCYP83A1. The McrBC-PCR assay confirmed reduced methylation in the BoCYP83A1 promoter region. The function of BoCYP83A1 in MeJA-induced SFN biosynthesis was validated using RNA interference in broccoli. Further evidence demonstrated that the transcription factors BoMYB68 and BoIAA18 promote BoCYP83A1 expression by directly binding to its promoter. Our findings not only provide novel insights into the epigenetic regulation underlying MeJA-induced SFN accumulation in plants but also facilitate molecular breeding for the development of high-SFN crops.
para1: 蘿蔔硫素是一種被廣泛認為對健康有益的化合物，許多研究顯示它可能有助於預防某些疾病。這項研究探討了甲基茉莉酸（MeJA）如何影響蘿蔔硫素的積累，並且揭示了表觀遺傳調控的角色。這些發現可能為未來的健康促進策略提供新的思路，尤其是在開發富含蘿蔔硫素的作物方面，這對於改善飲食和增進健康都有潛在的好處。

蘿蔔硫素是一種被廣泛認為對健康有益的化合物，許多研究顯示它可能有助於預防某些疾病。這項研究探討了甲基茉莉酸（MeJA）如何影響蘿蔔硫素的積累，並且揭示了表觀遺傳調控的角色。這些發現可能為未來的健康促進策略提供新的思路，尤其是在開發富含蘿蔔硫素的作物方面，這對於改善飲食和增進健康都有潛在的好處。

這項研究的主題是探討甲基茉莉酸對青花椰菜中蘿蔔硫素積累的影響，特別是它如何透過表觀遺傳調控來改變基因的甲基化狀態。研究者們關心的是在不同處理條件下，青花椰菜的基因表達和甲基化變化，這些變化如何影響蘿蔔硫素的生成，從而為作物改良提供科學依據。

研究團隊對青花椰菜的花和葉進行了綜合分析，使用了轉錄組學、全基因組亞硫酸氫鹽測序和小RNA測序等技術。他們比較了在甲基茉莉酸處理和對照條件下的樣本，特別關注甲基化區域的變化。研究中還使用了5-氮胞苷來抑制DNA甲基化，觀察其對蘿蔔硫素含量的影響，這樣的設計有助於深入理解表觀遺傳調控的機制。

主要發現顯示，甲基茉莉酸處理後，青花椰菜中某些基因的甲基化水平發生了顯著變化，特別是在CHG上下文中。研究確認了BoCYP83A1基因在蘿蔔硫素生物合成中的重要性，並且發現轉錄因子BoMYB68和BoIAA18能夠直接結合到其啟動子上，促進該基因的表達。這些結果不僅增進了我們對植物中蘿蔔硫素積累的表觀遺傳調控的理解，還為高蘿蔔硫素作物的分子育種提供了新的方向。

title_en: A natural product-hybridization approach toward anticancer drug discovery: synthesis and antitumor evaluation of CTBC6, designed from sulforaphane and magnolol.
abstract_en: Natural products, with their promising drug-like profiles, inherent pharmacological potential, and a vast reservoir of privileged structures, have long been recognized as a rich source of hit compounds in anticancer drug discovery. Building upon our group's prior work, this study delves into the medicinal chemistry of sulforaphane (SFN)-a star molecule in anticancer research, leading to the discovery of CTBC6 as a novel small molecule anticancer compound. We demonstrated that CTBC6 exhibited potent anti-breast cancer effects both in vitro and in vivo with no significant organ toxicity. We investigated the anti-breast cancer function of the hit hybrid compound CTBC6, and found that CTBC6 induced apoptosis and caused cell cycle arrest at the G2/M phase, while also effectively suppressing cell migration. Moreover, transcriptome analysis and the mechanistic studies revealed that CTBC6 may impede breast cancer progression by inhibiting MEK/ERK signaling pathway. Importantly, the functional targets of CTBC6 in breast cancer was assessed. Through chemical proteomics, we identified CNOT10 and STAT5A as potential direct targets through which CTBC6 exerts its anti-breast cancer activity. Our study lays a good foundation for further medical research of sulforaphane and its derivatives, and also presents a new strategic paradigm for natural product-driven anticancer drug discovery.
para1: 這項研究對健康和疾病預防的啟示在於，天然產品可能成為抗癌藥物開發的重要來源。研究顯示，某些天然化合物如蘿蔔硫素，具有潛在的藥理特性，能夠影響癌細胞的生長和擴散。這意味著，透過深入研究這些天然化合物，我們或許能找到新的治療方法，幫助預防或治療癌症，特別是乳癌等常見疾病。這樣的研究不僅能促進我們對癌症機制的理解，也可能為未來的藥物開發提供新的思路和策略。
para2: 本研究的主題是探索蘿蔔硫素及其衍生物在抗癌方面的潛力，特別是針對乳癌的治療效果。研究團隊在之前的工作基礎上，進一步分析了一種新發現的小分子化合物CTBC6，並評估其對乳癌細胞的影響。研究的重點在於了解CTBC6如何作用於乳癌細胞，並尋找其可能的作用機制，這對於開發新的抗癌藥物具有重要意義。
para3: 在實驗設計上，研究團隊使用了體外和體內的實驗方法來評估CTBC6的抗乳癌效果。研究對象包括乳癌細胞和動物模型，通過這些實驗，團隊能夠觀察到CTBC6對癌細胞的影響，包括誘導細胞凋亡和阻止細胞週期進入G2/M階段。此外，研究還進行了轉錄組分析，以深入了解CTBC6的作用機制，這樣的多方位研究有助於全面評估其抗癌潛力。
para4: 主要發現顯示，CTBC6對乳癌細胞具有顯著的抑制作用，並且在實驗中未觀察到明顯的器官毒性。研究指出，CTBC6能夠通過抑制MEK/ERK信號通路來減緩乳癌的進展。此外，通過化學蛋白質組學的分析，研究團隊還識別了CNOT10和STAT5A作為CTBC6可能的直接靶點，這些發現為未來的研究提供了新的方向，並為天然產品驅動的抗癌藥物開發奠定了良好的基礎。

title_en: Xenobiotic Sulforaphane in Head and Neck Cancer: Beyond the Nrf2 Pathway.
abstract_en: Head and neck cancers (HNCs) represent a major global health burden and remain associated with substantial morbidity and limited therapeutic options, particularly in advanced or recurrent disease. Increasing interest has focused on naturally derived bioactive compounds with potential chemopreventive and therapeutic properties. Sulforaphane, a dietary xenobiotic isothiocyanate derived from glucoraphanin in cruciferous vegetables, has attracted attention due to its ability to modulate redox balance, epigenetic regulation, and multiple oncogenic signaling pathways. This manuscript reviews current evidence regarding the biological effects of sulforaphane in HNCs. Particular attention is given to the molecular mechanisms underlying its modulation of the nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) pathway, a key regulator of cellular antioxidant and detoxification responses that can be activated by sulforaphane. Several studies indicate that sulforaphane can inhibit tumor growth through several mechanisms beyond Nrf2 activation, including induction of apoptosis, cell cycle arrest, epigenetic modulation, and suppression of oncogenic signaling pathways. In addition, sulforaphane has been shown to enhance the efficacy of conventional treatments, including chemotherapy, radiotherapy, and photodynamic therapy. Overall, the literature suggests that sulforaphane may represent a promising chemopreventive or therapeutic adjunct in HNC, although further clinical investigation is required to clarify its translational potential.
para1: 頭頸癌是一種全球健康的重要議題，對患者的生活品質造成了很大的影響，尤其是在疾病進展或復發的情況下，治療選擇相對有限。因此，尋找能夠預防或輔助治療的天然化合物變得越來越重要。研究顯示，某些天然成分可能具有化學預防和治療的潛力，這對於改善頭頸癌患者的健康狀況或許能帶來新的希望。
para2: 這項研究主要關注的是蘿蔔硫素在頭頸癌中的生物效應。蘿蔔硫素是一種來自十字花科蔬菜的天然化合物，具有調節細胞內抗氧化和解毒反應的能力。研究者希望深入了解蘿蔔硫素如何影響頭頸癌的發展，特別是它在細胞內的作用機制，以及它是否能夠作為輔助治療的選擇。
para3: 在這項研究中，研究者回顧了現有的文獻，分析了蘿蔔硫素對頭頸癌的影響。研究重點放在蘿蔔硫素如何調節核因子紅血球2相關因子2（Nrf2）這一關鍵通路，這個通路對於細胞的抗氧化和解毒反應至關重要。此外，研究者也探討了蘿蔔硫素如何透過多種機制來抑制腫瘤生長，包括誘導細胞凋亡和細胞週期停滯等。
para4: 研究結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素可能透過多種機制來抑制頭頸癌的腫瘤生長，並且有潛力增強傳統治療的效果，例如化療、放療和光動力療法。雖然目前的文獻表明蘿蔔硫素在頭頸癌的化學預防或輔助治療中可能具有前景，但仍需進一步的臨床研究來確認其實際應用的潛力。

title_en: Targeting TLR4-Depended NLRP3 Inflammasome: Sulforaphane's Mechanism in Suppressing Neuroinflammation and Seizures in Epileptic Mice.
abstract_en: This study aimed to elucidate the role of the TLR4 pathway in the antiepileptic effects of sulforaphane (SFN). C57BL/6 mice were randomized into control, SFN, epilepsy (EP), and SFN intervention groups. SFN was administered for 7 days (25 mg/kg/day, oral gavage) to the intervention and SFN groups before epilepsy was induced. Epilepsy was induced in the EP and intervention groups using a lithium chloride-pilocarpine protocol. Behavioral and electroencephalogram (EEG) data were collected. Hippocampal tissue was examined for inflammatory markers and histopathological changes, and protein expression in the TLR4/NF‑κB/NLRP3 pathway was assessed by western blot. The involvement of TLR4 was further validated through intraperitoneal administration of the selective inhibitor TAK‑242 (3.0 mg/kg). SFN pretreatment significantly ameliorated seizure severity, mortality, pathologically elevated EEG delta wave frequency, hippocampal pro‑inflammatory cytokine levels, and neuronal damage (p < 0.05). Molecular analysis showed that SFN reduced the expression of TLR4, MyD88, p-NF-κB, NLRP3, and caspase-1 (p < 0.05). SFN combined with TAK-242 did not further enhance the reduction of these indicators, compared with SFN alone. The TLR4/NF-κB/NLRP3 signaling pathway plays a crucial role in SFN-mediated attenuation of neuroinflammation and contributes to its antiepileptic and neuroprotective effects.

para2: 這項研究的主題是探討TLR4通路在蘿蔔硫素抗癲癇效果中的角色。研究者希望了解蘿蔔硫素如何透過這個特定的生物通路來減輕癲癇發作的影響。癲癇是一種常見的神經系統疾病，對患者的生活造成重大影響，因此深入研究其潛在的治療方法是非常重要的。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者使用了C57BL/6小鼠，將它們隨機分為對照組、蘿蔔硫素組、癲癇組和蘿蔔硫素介入組。蘿蔔硫素以口服方式給予介入組和蘿蔔硫素組，持續七天，然後在癲癇組和介入組中誘發癲癇。研究者收集了行為和腦電圖（EEG）數據，並檢查了海馬體的炎症標記和組織病理變化，還評估了TLR4/NF-κB/NLRP3通路中的蛋白質表達。
para4: 主要發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素的預處理顯著減輕了癲癇發作的嚴重程度、死亡率、病理性升高的EEG delta波頻率、海馬體的促炎細胞因子水平以及神經損傷。分子分析結果顯示，蘿蔔硫素降低了TLR4、MyD88、p-NF-κB、NLRP3和caspase-1的表達。儘管蘿蔔硫素與選擇性抑制劑TAK-242聯合使用，但並未進一步增強這些指標的降低效果，顯示出TLR4/NF-κB/NLRP3信號通路在蘿蔔硫素減輕神經炎症及其抗癲癇和神經保護效果中的重要性。

title_en: Sulforaphane Attenuates PM<sub>2.5</sub>-Induced Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease by Modulation of Nrf2 Activating and EGFR/PI3K/AKT Signaling.
abstract_en: This study aimed to investigate the prophylactic and therapeutic effects of Sulforaphane (SFN) on PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and to elucidate the underlying mechanisms. A PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced COPD rat model was established. The protective effects of SFN, administered concurrently with PM<sub>2.5</sub> exposure, were evaluated by analyzing inflammatory cytokine levels and performing histopathological examinations of lung tissues. The antioxidant role of SFN in COPD was further investigated both in vivo and in vitro via the Nrf2 signaling. Subsequently, potential targets and underlying mechanisms of SFN in COPD treatment were predicted through network pharmacological analyses. Molecular docking simulations were then performed to validate the binding affinities between SFN and key target proteins. Furthermore, the modulatory effects of SFN on the EGFR/PI3K/AKT signaling in PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced COPD were investigated both in vivo and in vitro, and EGFR silencing was subsequently used to confirm the involvement of this pathway. Finally, the therapeutic efficacy of SFN in attenuating PM<sub>2.5</sub>-exacerbated COPD progression was evaluated through histopathological examination and assessment of Nrf2 and EGFR/PI3K/AKT pathway activation. SFN significantly attenuated PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced lung injury, inflammation, mucus hypersecretion, and redox stress. Mechanistically, SFN directly activated the Nrf2 signaling, reduced reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation, and thereby mitigated COPD progression. Integrated network pharmacology and molecular docking analyses further identified EGFR as a potential key target of SFN. Consistently, SFN could directly bind to EGFR and suppress EGFR/PI3K/AKT signaling, contributing to its prophylactic effects. Moreover, in the rat AECOPD model, SFN exerted therapeutic benefits primarily via EGFR/PI3K/AKT inhibition, with minimal involvement of Nrf2-driven antioxidant effects. Here, these findings reveal a dual mechanism by which SFN attenuates PM<sub>2.5</sub>-induced COPD via Nrf2 activation and inhibition of EGFR/PI3K/AKT signaling, thereby highlighting its potential as a promising candidate for COPD and its acute exacerbation.

title_en: Sulforaphane promotes the removal of doxorubicin-induced senescent epithelial and breast cancer cells and activates AhR/CYP1A1-based adaptive response in vitro.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane (SFN), an isothiocyanate derived from cruciferous vegetables, is characterized by antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties by activating NRF2 pathway, suppressing NFκB pathway, and promoting apoptotic cell death signals and epigenetic changes, respectively. However, SFN-based action against drug-induced senescent cells has never been addressed. In the present study, three breast cancer cell lines with different receptor status, namely triple negative BT-549 and HCC38, and MDA-MB-361 (ER-positive, HER2-positive) along with normal epithelial MCF-10F cells were subjected to 5 μM SFN treatment at both proliferating and non-proliferating (doxorubicin-induced senescence) state. BT-549 proliferating cells were more prone to SFN stimulation than normal ones that was accompanied by oxidative stress-based apoptosis. In contrast, SFN was active in a similar manner against both drug-induced senescent normal and breast cancer cells that resulted in apoptosis-mediated elimination of non-proliferating cells. SFN also stimulated adaptive responses in doxorubicin-induced senescent cells as judged by the activation of aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AhR)/cytochrome P450 1A1 (CYP1A1) pathway and upregulation of heat shock protein 90 (HSP90). In silico analysis with molecular docking also predicted SFN interactions with selected proteins involved in drug detoxification and cell death that warrants further investigation. We postulate that SFN may have senotherapeutic potential, especially against apoptosis resistant chemotherapy-induced senescent normal and breast cancer cells.
para1: 這項研究可能對健康和疾病預防提供了一些啟示，特別是在癌症治療方面。蘿蔔硫素是一種來自十字花科蔬菜的化合物，具有抗氧化、抗發炎和抗癌的特性。這些特性使得蘿蔔硫素在對抗與藥物相關的老化細胞方面顯得特別重要。隨著癌症治療的進步，了解如何利用這些天然化合物來改善治療效果，可能會對未來的癌症治療策略產生積極影響。
para2: 本研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對於藥物誘導的老化細胞的作用，特別是針對不同類型的乳癌細胞。研究者關心的是蘿蔔硫素是否能夠有效地促進這些老化細胞的死亡，並且評估其在不同細胞狀態下的效果。這項研究不僅涉及癌細胞，還包括正常的上皮細胞，以便比較它們對蘿蔔硫素的反應。
para3: 在實驗中，研究者選用了三種不同受體狀態的乳癌細胞系，包括三陰性細胞BT-549和HCC38，以及ER陽性、HER2陽性的MDA-MB-361，還有正常的上皮細胞MCF-10F。這些細胞在增殖和非增殖狀態下（如藥物誘導的老化狀態）接受了5μM的蘿蔔硫素處理。研究者觀察了這些細胞在不同處理下的反應，特別是對於蘿蔔硫素的刺激反應及其引發的細胞死亡機制。
para4: 主要的發現是，BT-549增殖細胞對蘿蔔硫素的刺激反應較正常細胞強，並伴隨著氧化壓力引發的細胞死亡。相對而言，蘿蔔硫素對於藥物誘導的老化正常細胞和乳癌細胞的作用相似，均能促進細胞死亡。此外，蘿蔔硫素還能激活與藥物解毒和細胞死亡相關的特定蛋白質，這些結果顯示蘿蔔硫素可能在對抗抗藥性老化細胞方面具有潛力，但仍需進一步研究來確認其具體機制。

title_en: Restoration of Immune Surveillance in Prostate Cancer Prevention by Sulforaphane in Hi-Myc Mice.
abstract_en: Oral administration of broccoli constituent sulforaphane (SFN) prevents prostate cancer development in preclinical mouse models. However, the mechanism(s) underlying prostate cancer prevention by SFN are not fully understood. In this study, we used a human relevant mouse model (Hi-Myc mice) to demonstrate restoration of immune surveillance by oral SFN administration. Treatment of Hi-Myc mice with SFN for 16 weeks resulted in about 1.33-fold increase in the number of prostate tumor-infiltrating CD8α + T cells (p = 0.02 by Student's t-test). The number of CD4+ helper T cells was not affected by SFN treatment. The number of CD11c/MHCII+ dendritic cells was increased by about 57% upon SFN administration. On the other hand, the number of NKp46+ natural killer cells was not significantly affected by SFN treatment. Oral administration of SFN resulted in about 30% decrease in the number of Gr1/CD11b+ myeloid-derived suppressor cells in the prostate tumor when compared to control mice. Plasma levels of interleukin (IL)-1α, IL-1β, IL-4, IL-5, IL-10, and C-X-C motif chemokine ligand 2 (CXCL2 or MIP-2) were statistically significantly lower in SFN-treated mice when compared to control mice. Treatment of recurrent prostate cancer patients with 200 μmol/day of SFN-rich broccoli sprout extract for 20 weeks also caused a statistically significant decrease in plasma levels of IL-1β, IL-4, and IL-13. Cell proliferation inhibition by SFN in vitro was partially but significantly attenuated by IL-4 and IL-13 supplementation in 22Rv1 cells. These results indicate restoration of immune surveillance by oral SFN treatment in Hi-Myc mouse model.

title_en: Sulforaphane Enhances Cytotoxic Effects of Non-Thermal Plasma and Tirapazamine Combination Therapy in Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma Cells.
abstract_en: Pancreatic adenocarcinoma remains a highly lethal malignancy with limited effective treatment options, largely due to late-stage detection and rapid progression to metastatic disease. Therapeutic strategies capable of targeting both pre-metastatic and metastatic tumors are critically needed. In this study, we evaluated a combination therapy consisting of non-thermal plasma (NTP), a generator of reactive oxygen and nitrogen species, and the hypoxia-activated prodrug tirapazamine (TPZ). We further investigated whether sulforaphane (SF), a bioactive phytochemical, could further enhance therapeutic efficacy. NTP and TPZ produced strong cytotoxic effects as single agents and demonstrated additive to synergistic activity when combined, reducing viability by 87% in pre-metastatic BxPC-3 cells and achieving near-complete elimination of metastatic AsPC-1 cells. The addition of sulforaphane (10 µM) further enhanced cytotoxicity across all treatment conditions, with Bliss independence analysis indicating additive to synergistic interactions depending on cell line and treatment combination. Sulforaphane-mediated enhancement occurred without restoration of connexin 43 expression or coordinated reversal of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition markers, and treatments did not induce N-cadherin upregulation or suggest acquisition of invasive characteristics. Together, these findings support NTP + TPZ as a potent combinatorial strategy for pancreatic adenocarcinoma and identify sulforaphane as an effective adjunct that enhances cytotoxic efficacy through mechanisms that remain to be fully elucidated.

para4: 主要的研究發現顯示，NTP和TPZ的結合能顯著降低腫瘤細胞的存活率，對於早期的BxPC-3細胞降低了87%的存活率，而對轉移性的AsPC-1細胞則幾乎完全消滅。當加入蘿蔔硫素後，所有治療條件下的細胞毒性都進一步增強，顯示出不同細胞系和治療組合之間的協同作用。儘管這些治療方法的效果顯著，但研究也指出，這些增強作用的具體機制仍需進一步探討。

title_en: Sulforaphane Alleviates Zearalenone-Induced Oxidative Stress in Bovine Mammary Epithelial Cells.
journal: Animals : an open access journal from MDPI
abstract_en: Zearalenone (ZEA) is a common contaminant in crops and animal feed. However, research on the effects of ZEA on animal mammary tissue is relatively limited. Sulforaphane (SFN) is a naturally active compound mainly derived from cruciferous vegetables (such as broccoli), with significant antioxidant and cytoprotective effects. The purpose of this study is the effect of SFN on ZEA-induced toxicity in bovine mammary epithelial cells (MAC-T). By treating MAC-T cells with different concentrations of ZEA and SFN for 24 h, the results showed that different concentrations of ZEA (10, 20, 40, 60, 80, or 100 μM) could inhibit MAC-T cell viability. Treatment with SFN at concentrations of 1, 2.5, and 5 μM had no significant effect on cell viability. The results of combined treatment with 10 μM ZEA and 1, 2.5, or 5 μM SFN showed that SFN could significantly reverse the decrease in cell viability caused by ZEA; reduce the increase in lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) release, reactive oxygen species (ROS), and malondialdehyde (MDA) content induced by ZEA; and increase the levels of glutathione (GSH), superoxide dismutase (SOD), and mitochondrial membrane potential that were decreased by ZEA. SFN can significantly inhibit the upregulation of interleukin 6 (IL-6), tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-α), and interleukin 1 beta (IL-1β) induced by ZEA exposure and markedly reverse the increase in cell apoptosis rate caused by ZEA. Compared with the control group, the expression of genes nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (Nrf2), heme oxygenase 1 (HO-1), NAD(P)H:quinone oxidoreductase 1 (NQO1), glutamate-cysteine ligase modifier subunit (GCLM), and glutathione peroxidase 1 (GPX1) was significantly reduced in the ZEA group, while the addition of SFN effectively increased the expression levels of these genes. Corresponding protein detection results were consistent with the trends in gene expression. This study demonstrated that SFN alleviates ZEA-induced damage to MAC-T cells by activating the Nrf2 pathway, providing a theoretical basis for the subsequent application of SFN in dairy farming to prevent and control breast health risks related to mycotoxins.
para1: 這項研究對健康或疾病預防的啟示在於，蘿蔔硫素可能有助於減輕由於農作物污染物（如玉米赤霉烯酮）引起的乳腺組織損傷。這對於乳牛的健康管理特別重要，因為乳腺健康直接影響到乳製品的品質與安全性。透過了解蘿蔔硫素的保護作用，未來在乳品生產中，農民或許可以利用這種天然化合物來降低毒素對乳牛的影響，進而提升乳製品的安全性與消費者的健康保障。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對玉米赤霉烯酮引起的毒性影響，特別是在牛的乳腺上皮細胞中的作用。研究者關心的是，這種常見的農作物污染物如何影響乳腺細胞的生存與健康，以及蘿蔔硫素是否能夠有效減輕這種毒性。這不僅涉及到動物的健康問題，也與食品安全密切相關，因此具有重要的實用價值。
para3: 實驗中，研究者使用了牛的乳腺上皮細胞（MAC-T）作為研究對象，並對這些細胞進行了不同濃度的玉米赤霉烯酮和蘿蔔硫素處理，持續24小時。具體來說，他們測試了不同濃度的玉米赤霉烯酮（從10到100 μM不等）對細胞存活率的影響，同時也觀察了不同濃度的蘿蔔硫素（1、2.5和5 μM）對細胞的保護作用。這樣的設計有助於評估蘿蔔硫素是否能夠逆轉玉米赤霉烯酮造成的細胞損傷。
para4: 主要的研究發現顯示，玉米赤霉烯酮確實會抑制乳腺上皮細胞的存活率，而蘿蔔硫素的添加則能顯著改善這一情況。研究結果表明，蘿蔔硫素不僅能減少由玉米赤霉烯酮引起的細胞死亡，還能降低細胞內的氧化壓力和炎症反應。此外，蘿蔔硫素能夠促進一些與抗氧化和細胞保護相關的基因表達，這些結果為未來在乳品生產中應用蘿蔔硫素提供了理論基礎。

title_en: Sulforaphane promotes mammary epithelial cell proliferation in Xizang sheep by increasing FOXM1 and PI3K-Akt signaling.
abstract_en: Mammary epithelial cells (MECs) are crucial for milk synthesis and secretion, and their abundance and activity directly influence milk yield and quality. This study investigates the effects of sulforaphane (SFN) on MECs isolated from Xizang Sewa sheep. The findings reveal that SFN at a concentration of 5 μM promotes cell proliferation, enhances DNA synthesis, facilitates the G1/S phase transition, and increases the expression levels of critical cell cycle regulators such as Ki67, PCNA, CDK4, CCNE1, CCNA2, and CCND2. RNA-sequencing analysis further confirms the modulation of numerous genes involved in cell cycle regulation, including FOXM1. Further investigation into FOXM1 demonstrates that its inhibition results in a significant reduction in the mRNA and protein levels of various cell cycle regulatory genes induced by SFN, including Ki67, PCNA, CDK4, and CCNE1. Furthermore, treatment with a PI3K-Akt signaling inhibitor (LY294002) leads to a decrease in PI3K-Akt signaling activity, concomitant with reduced levels of PCNA and FOXM1. However, these effects are ameliorated following SFN treatment. These findings suggest that SFN may significantly enhance cell proliferation of MECs in sheep by upregulating FOXM1 expression and activating the PI3K-AKT signaling pathway. This study identifies a promising pharmacological target for augmenting milk production in sheep.
para1: 這項研究可能對健康和疾病預防有一些啟示，特別是在乳製品生產方面。研究顯示，透過促進乳腺上皮細胞的增殖，可能有助於提高乳量和乳品質。這對於農業和乳品產業來說，能夠提供新的方法來提升生產效率，進而影響到人類的飲食和營養來源。若能有效提升乳製品的產量，將有助於滿足日益增長的需求，並可能改善相關的健康問題。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討蘿蔔硫素對於乳腺上皮細胞的影響。研究者關心的是這種化合物如何影響來自西藏的羊的乳腺上皮細胞，特別是它對細胞增殖和基因表達的影響。透過這項研究，科學家希望能夠了解蘿蔔硫素在乳製品生產中的潛在應用，並尋找提升乳量的新策略。
para3: 實驗中，研究者從西藏的羊中分離出乳腺上皮細胞，並使用不同濃度的蘿蔔硫素進行處理。研究主要觀察這些細胞的增殖情況、DNA合成以及細胞週期的變化。研究者還進行了RNA測序分析，以確認與細胞週期調控相關的基因表達變化，並進一步探討了FOXM1基因的作用，這是一個在細胞增殖中扮演重要角色的基因。
para4: 主要發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素能夠促進乳腺上皮細胞的增殖，並提高多個細胞週期調控基因的表達水平。研究還發現，抑制FOXM1會顯著降低這些基因的表達，顯示FOXM1在蘿蔔硫素促進細胞增殖中的關鍵角色。此外，使用PI3K-Akt信號通路抑制劑的實驗結果顯示，這條信號通路的活性與細胞增殖有關，並且蘿蔔硫素的處理能夠改善這些影響。這些結果表明，蘿蔔硫素可能透過上調FOXM1的表達和激活PI3K-AKT信號通路來顯著促進羊的乳腺上皮細胞增殖。

title_en: Sulforaphane Synergies with Phytochemicals and Pharmaceuticals: Implications for Healthspan.
abstract_en: Sulforaphane, a bioactive isothiocyanate found abundantly in cruciferous vegetables, has attracted significant attention for its chemopreventive and therapeutic potential, particularly in cancer. There is now an abundance of peer-reviewed research documenting true synergies between sulforaphane and (a) cancer treatment drugs, (b) pharmaceuticals in development but not yet on the market or in the regulatory pipeline, (c) other phytochemicals, and (d) proprietary mixtures such as leaf extracts and other botanicals, as well as evidence that some cell lines resistant to various cancer drugs become more susceptible when treated with sulforaphane. Most of the published studies demonstrate evidence for synergy in cancer, including cancers of the bladder, blood, brain, breast, colon, esophagus, liver, lung, ovaries, prostate, and skin, where reducing drug dosages could yield substantial patient benefits. Importantly, non-cancer indications have also been reported, such as mitigation of cardiac toxicity, inflammation, obesity, and pain (including antihyperalgesic and antinociceptive effects). Synergistic effects are most often demonstrated in cell line models, with many studies providing robust mechanistic evidence, and some employing the gold-standard Chou-Talalay method for quantifying synergy. Current evidence on the synergistic interactions of sulforaphane with both phytochemicals and pharmaceuticals highlights underlying mechanisms such as modulation of oxidative stress, inflammation, apoptosis, and epigenetic regulation, suggesting significant clinical and therapeutic implications. By providing a comprehensive overview of sulforaphane synergies in both cancer and non-cancer contexts, we aim to inform future research and support the development of integrated therapeutic strategies.
para1: 蘿蔔硫素是一種在十字花科蔬菜中豐富的生物活性化合物，近年來受到廣泛關注，因為它可能對健康有益，尤其是在癌症的預防和治療方面。研究顯示，蘿蔔硫素與癌症治療藥物之間存在協同作用，這意味著它可能能增強藥物的效果，甚至讓一些對藥物抵抗的癌細胞變得更容易受到治療。這些發現不僅對癌症患者有潛在的好處，還可能對心臟毒性、炎症、肥胖和疼痛等非癌症相關的健康問題有所幫助，因此，了解蘿蔔硫素的作用機制對於未來的健康策略非常重要。
para2: 這項研究主要關注蘿蔔硫素在癌症及其他健康問題中的協同作用。研究者們希望了解蘿蔔硫素如何與各種藥物和植物化學物質互相作用，並探討這些互動對癌症治療的潛在影響。特別是，研究中提到的癌症類型包括膀胱癌、血癌、腦癌、乳癌、結腸癌、食道癌、肝癌、肺癌、卵巢癌、前列腺癌和皮膚癌等，這顯示出蘿蔔硫素的研究範圍相當廣泛。
para3: 在實驗方面，研究者們主要使用細胞株模型來探討蘿蔔硫素的作用。他們採用了多種方法來評估蘿蔔硫素與其他藥物和植物化學物質的協同作用，並且一些研究還使用了被認為是黃金標準的Chou-Talalay方法來量化這些協同效應。這些實驗不僅提供了關於蘿蔔硫素如何影響癌細胞的機制證據，還揭示了其在非癌症相關健康問題中的潛在應用。
para4: 主要的研究發現顯示，蘿蔔硫素與多種藥物和植物化學物質之間存在顯著的協同作用，這可能有助於降低癌症治療中的藥物劑量，從而減少患者的副作用。此外，研究還指出，蘿蔔硫素在減輕心臟毒性、炎症、肥胖和疼痛等方面也可能具有潛在的益處。這些結果強調了蘿蔔硫素在臨床和治療上的重要性，並為未來的研究提供了指導方向。

title_en: Purple Light Steers Metabolic Flux Towards Sulforaphane in Flowering Chinese Cabbage Sprouts by Activating a BcHY5-Mediated "Push-Pull" Mechanism.
abstract_en: Light quality is a powerful tool to modulate the chemistry of functional foods, yet the underlying molecular regulatory networks are poorly understood. This study reveals that purple light-emitting diode illumination engineers the metabolic flux in flowering Chinese cabbage sprouts to maximize the production of the anti-cancer agent sulforaphane. Compared to dark and white light controls, purple light treatment (center wavelength: 398 nm, half-width: 13 nm) resulted in a significant (6.84-fold) increase in sulforaphane content. Integrated metabolomic and transcriptomic analyses uncovered a "push-pull" mechanism: purple light "pushed" glucosinolate biosynthesis while "pulling" the metabolic stream towards sulforaphane by enhancing hydrolysis and suppressing the competing nitrile-forming pathway. We identified the transcription factor BcHY5 as the master regulator. Yeast one-hybrid and dual-luciferase assays confirmed BcHY5 directly binds to and activates promoters of key GSL biosynthetic genes (GSTU11, SUR1). These findings establish a novel light-regulatory framework, offering a practical strategy for the precision production of high-value phytochemicals in functional foods.

title_en: Oxidative Stress in Keratoconus Is Evident in Tear Fluid and Stromal Cells and Alleviated in Cell Culture by Sulforaphane.
abstract_en: Keratoconus (KC) is a common eye disease characterized by progressive corneal thinning and steepening. Despite multiple treatment options, there is no definitive cure for KC. Previously we identified loss and dysregulation of nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2 (NRF2) mediated antioxidant functions in stromal cells and extracellular matrix (ECM) in KC. Here we used tear fluid samples and cell culture models to investigate oxidative stress in KC. Primary human KC and donor (DN) stromal fibroblasts were exposed to hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) to induce oxidative stress and treated with sulforaphane (SFN) for antioxidant rescue. The fibroblasts were then assessed for NRF2 activation and apoptosis by measuring TXNRD1, HMOX1, NRF2, and GPX3 expression and caspase-3/7 activity. ML385 was used to inhibit NRF2 functions in DN fibroblast cultures followed by measurements of cell death (Caspase 3/7), proliferation (BrdU and Ki-67 labeling) and ECM deposition by immunohistology. Oxidative stress was directly assessed in KC and non-KC subjects by measuring malondialdehyde (MDA) and glutathione peroxidase 3 (GPX3) levels in the tear fluid. H2O2-stressed KC fibroblasts displayed increased apoptosis and suboptimal NRF2 activation, which could be rescued with SFN. Conversely, DN fibroblasts treated with ML385 elicited KC-like cellular phenotypes, including decreased antioxidant response, reduced cell growth, myofibroblastic changes and poor ECM deposition. Compared to unaffected controls, KC patient tear fluid exhibited elevated levels of GPX3 and MDA, a byproduct of lipid peroxidation. NRF2-mediated anti-oxidative functions are dysregulated in KC. In the future SFN antioxidant treatments may be therapeutic in KC, while MDA and GPX3 may lead to promising biomarkers for diagnosis and severity predictions.
para1: 角膜圓錐症（Keratoconus, KC）是一種常見的眼疾，主要特徵是角膜逐漸變薄和變形。雖然目前有多種治療選擇，但仍然沒有明確的治癒方法。這項研究的發現可能對健康和疾病預防提供一些啟示，特別是在了解氧化壓力如何影響角膜圓錐症的發展方面。透過研究與抗氧化劑相關的機制，未來或許能找到更有效的治療策略，改善患者的生活品質，並可能在早期診斷和預測疾病嚴重程度上提供幫助。
para2: 這項研究的主題是探討角膜圓錐症患者的氧化壓力及其對細胞的影響。研究者關心的是在這種眼疾中，細胞內的抗氧化功能如何受到損害，以及這種損害如何與疾病的進展相關。特別是，研究著重於核因子紅血球2相關因子2（NRF2）在角膜圓錐症中的角色，並試圖了解其在細胞中的表現和功能是否受到影響。
para3: 研究中使用了眼淚樣本和細胞培養模型來進行實驗。主要的實驗對象是來自角膜圓錐症患者的原代角膜基質成纖維細胞和健康捐贈者的成纖維細胞。研究者將這些細胞暴露於過氧化氫（H2O2）以誘導氧化壓力，並使用蘿蔔硫素（SFN）進行抗氧化治療。隨後，研究者評估了NRF2的活化情況和細胞凋亡，並測量了相關基因的表現和細胞死亡的活性。
para4: 主要的發現顯示，受到H2O2影響的角膜圓錐症成纖維細胞顯示出增加的細胞凋亡和NRF2活化不足的情況，而這些問題可以透過蘿蔔硫素得到改善。相對地，使用ML385抑制NRF2功能的健康捐贈者成纖維細胞則出現了類似角膜圓錐症的細胞特徵，包括抗氧化反應減弱和細胞增殖下降。此外，角膜圓錐症患者的眼淚樣本中，GPX3和MDA的水平顯著升高，這些指標可能成為未來診斷和預測疾病嚴重程度的潛在生物標記。
